title: Final verification: grep for remaining old token references
description:
## Task
Run a comprehensive grep across the entire codebase to confirm zero remaining old/flat token references in production code. Check for:

1. `var(--deep-navy)` in any .css or .tsx file (excluding tokens.css :root definitions)
2. `var(--cool-gray)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
3. `var(--ocean-blue)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
4. `var(--coral)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
5. `var(--mint-fresh)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
6. `var(--warm-gold)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
7. `var(--light-gray)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
8. `var(--warm-white)` (excluding tokens.css :root)
9. `var(--surface-primary)` without color- prefix (excluding tokens.css :root)
10. `var(--border-subtle)` without color- prefix (excluding tokens.css :root)
11. `var(--text-primary)` without color- prefix
12. `var(--text-secondary)` without color- prefix
13. Hardcoded hex: #1A1A2E, #6B7280, #E8EFF5, #1A2B3D, #64748B in .css/.tsx (excluding tokens.css :root and dark-mode-test.css)

Report findings. If any remain, flag them as gaps.

## Test Steps
1. Each grep pattern returns 0 matches in production code
2. Only allowed exceptions: tokens.css :root brand definitions, dark-mode-test.css intentional test values
3. Run `pnpm run types` - passes
4. Run `pnpm run dev` - no console errors about undefined CSS variables

## Execution
Execute this task directly. When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"`
2. Close the bead: `bd close <your-id>`

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work that's clearly needed:
```bash
bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"
```

## Capturing Learnings
If you encounter something noteworthy:
```bash
bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"
```

title: Update tokens.css utility classes to semantic token names
description:
## Task
In `src/design-system/tokens.css`, update the utility classes at the bottom of the file to use semantic token names instead of flat names:

- `.heading-5xl` through `.heading-lg`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.body-lg`, `.body-md`, `.body-sm`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.label-lg`, `.label-md`, `.label-sm`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.caption`: change `color: var(--cool-gray)` → `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`
- `.text-primary`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)`
- `.text-secondary`: change `color: var(--cool-gray)` → `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`
- `.status-badge`: change `color: var(--deep-navy)` → `color: var(--color-text-primary)` and `background: var(--light-gray)` → `background: var(--color-surface-secondary)`
- `.status-badge--pending`: `color: var(--warm-gold)` → `color: var(--color-status-warning)`
- `.status-badge--completed`: `color: var(--mint-fresh)` → `color: var(--color-status-success)`
- `.status-badge--cancelled`: `color: var(--coral)` → `color: var(--color-status-error)`
- `.info-box--warning`: `border-color: var(--warm-gold)` → `border-color: var(--color-status-warning)`
- `.info-box--error`: `border-color: var(--coral)` → `border-color: var(--color-status-error)`
- `.info-box--success`: `border-color: var(--mint-fresh)` → `border-color: var(--color-status-success)`
- `.text-order-number`: `color: var(--ocean-blue)` → `color: var(--color-action-primary)`
- `.btn--outline:hover`: `border-color: var(--ocean-blue)` → `border-color: var(--color-action-primary)`
- Input placeholder: `color: var(--cool-gray)` → `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`

## Test Steps
1. Open tokens.css and verify no `var(--deep-navy)`, `var(--cool-gray)`, `var(--ocean-blue)`, `var(--coral)`, `var(--mint-fresh)`, `var(--warm-gold)`, `var(--light-gray)` remain in utility classes
2. Run `pnpm run types` - passes
3. Visual check: headings, body text, badges, buttons still render correctly

## Execution
Execute this task directly. When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"`
2. Close the bead: `bd close <your-id>`

title: P2 Medium: Data Integrity & Consistency
description:
## Task

Fix 4 medium-priority data integrity and consistency issues.

### 1. Optimize user loading in middleware
**File:** src/worker.tsx
- Currently loads user with ALL memberships + organizations on every request
- Change include to only fetch membership matching session's currentOrganizationId
- Fall back to all memberships only when org context is missing (first login)
- Verify all pages that need user.memberships still work

### 2. Filter soft-deleted users from order queries
**Files:** src/app/pages/admin/AdminOrdersPage.tsx (and other order queries)
- Soft-deleted users' orders still appear in admin views
- Audit all order queries in admin pages
- Add where clause or post-filter for user.deletedAt IS NULL
- Consider Prisma middleware for global soft-delete filtering

### 3. Validate invite email on accept
**File:** src/app/pages/admin/team/team-functions.ts
- Anyone with invite link can accept regardless of email match
- In acceptInvite(), compare ctx.user.email with invite.email
- If mismatch, reject or warn (prefer reject with clear message)
- Add invite.email display to AcceptInvitePage so user sees intended recipient

### 4. Use hasAdminAccess(ctx) consistently
**Files:** src/layouts/AdminLayout.tsx, AdminLayoutClient.tsx, and others
- AdminLayout.tsx uses raw string comparison ['owner', 'manager'].includes()
- Replace with hasAdminAccess(ctx) from src/utils/permissions.ts
- Audit AdminLayoutClient.tsx for similar inline checks
- Search codebase for other raw role string comparisons and fix

## Test Steps
1. Login and navigate all pages → everything still works
2. Verify first-login auto-org-selection still works
3. Soft-delete a user with orders → admin orders page hides/marks their orders
4. Create invite for user@example.com, accept as different@example.com → rejected
5. Accept as user@example.com → succeeds
6. Verify admin layout still grants/denies access correctly after permission refactor
7. Run pnpm run types → no errors

## Context Window Management
You have a large context window. Work through all 4 sub-tasks sequentially. However:
- **If you reach ~40% context usage and still have remaining sub-tasks**, stop cleanly:
  1. Commit completed work
  2. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed sub-tasks 1-N. Remaining: sub-tasks M-4"`
  3. Create a continuation bead for remaining work: `bd --no-daemon create "P2 Medium: Data Integrity (continued)" --description "<remaining tasks + test steps>" --assignee ralph --labels functional`
  4. Close this bead: `bd close <your-id>`
- **If you complete all sub-tasks within context**, commit all work, add summary comment, and close normally.

## Execution
When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"`
2. Close the bead: `bd close <your-id>`

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work: `bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"`

## Capturing Learnings
If you encounter something noteworthy: `bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"`

comments.author: ptre
comments.text: [summary] Completed all 4 sub-tasks: (1) Optimized middleware user loading to only fetch matching org membership when session has org context, falling back to all on first login. (2) Added soft-delete filter (user.deletedAt: null) to AdminOrdersPage and PrintOrdersPage queries. (3) Added email validation in acceptInvite() - rejects if invite.email doesn't match logged-in user's email; also shows invite email in AcceptInviteUI. (4) Replaced raw role string check in AdminLayoutClient with isOwner prop computed server-side via permissions.ts. UserMenu inline check left as-is (client component, prop-drilling not worth it). Types pass clean.

comments.author: ptre
comments.text: [GAP] UserMenu admin check - UserMenu.tsx:28-31 still has inline ['owner','manager'].includes() check identical to hasAdminAccess(). Would need prop-drilling through CustomerLayout→CustomerLayoutClient→Header→UserMenu to fix. Low priority since logic is correct.

title: Update patterns.md with semantic token naming convention
description:
## Task
Update `src/design-system/patterns.md` to document the three-tier semantic token system:

1. Add a section explaining the three-tier architecture: Brand → Alias → Component
2. Document the naming convention:
   - Colors: `--color-{category}-{variant}` (e.g., `--color-text-primary`, `--color-action-primary`, `--color-surface-secondary`)
   - Font sizes: `--font-size-{scale}` (e.g., `--font-size-sm`, `--font-size-xl`) — no shorthand `--text-sm`
   - Motion: `--duration-{speed}`, `--ease-out`
3. List all semantic aliases with their purpose
4. Note that brand-layer tokens (--deep-navy, --ocean-blue, etc.) still exist in :root but should NOT be used directly in components — always use the semantic alias layer
5. Reference `tokens-three-tier.json` as the source of truth

## Test Steps
1. Read updated patterns.md - naming convention is clear and complete
2. All documented token names match what's defined in tokens.css
3. No references to old flat token usage patterns remain in the docs
4. Examples use semantic names, not flat names

## Execution

title: P1 High: Schema & Consistency Fixes
description:
## Task

Fix 4 high-priority issues: schema constraints, indexes, error page consistency, and order state documentation.

### 1. Fix Credential @unique on userId
**File:** prisma/schema.prisma
- Remove @unique from userId in Credential model (keep @@index)
- Create migration: DROP unique index on userId
- WebAuthn expects 1-to-many (multiple devices per user)
- Verify credential lookups still work (they query by credentialId)

### 2. Add Market(organizationId, active) compound index
**File:** prisma/schema.prisma
- Add @@index([organizationId, active]) to Market model
- Create migration with CREATE INDEX
- Keep individual indexes (they serve other queries)

### 3. Standardize error/access-denied pages
**Files:** src/app/pages/admin/*.tsx, src/app/pages/admin/components/
- Currently 4 different patterns: inline Login, styled error div, dedicated component, access denied card
- Create shared AccessDenied and NotAuthenticated components in admin/components/
- Standardize on error-card CSS pattern already in admin.css
- Replace all inline error handling in admin pages

### 4. Document and validate order pricing state machine
**Files:** prisma/schema.prisma, src/utils/payments.ts, order server functions
- Document expected order lifecycle states and valid field combinations
- Add validation in server functions that create/update orders
- Ensure getPaymentStatus() covers all valid states
- Add comments to schema.prisma documenting field semantics

## Test Steps
1. Apply credential migration, verify registration still works
2. Apply market index migration, verify home page loads markets
3. Visit each admin page as unauthenticated → same error UI everywhere
4. Visit each admin page as customer → same access denied UI
5. Create order → verify required fields populated
6. Process payment → verify valid state transition
7. Run pnpm run types → no errors

## Context Window Management
You have a large context window. Work through all 4 sub-tasks sequentially. However:
- **If you reach ~40% context usage and still have remaining sub-tasks**, stop cleanly:
  1. Commit completed work
  2. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed sub-tasks 1-N. Remaining: sub-tasks M-4"`
  3. Create a continuation bead for remaining work: `bd --no-daemon create "P1 High: Schema & Consistency (continued)" --description "<remaining tasks + test steps>" --assignee ralph --labels functional`
  4. Close this bead: `bd close <your-id>`
- **If you complete all sub-tasks within context**, commit all work, add summary comment, and close normally.

## Execution
When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"`
2. Close the bead: `bd close <your-id>`

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work: `bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"`

## Capturing Learnings
If you encounter something noteworthy: `bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"`

comments.author: ptre
comments.text: [summary] Completed all 4 sub-tasks: (1) Removed @unique from Credential.userId, created migration to drop unique index — allows multiple passkeys per user. (2) Added Market(organizationId, active) compound index in schema + migration. (3) Created shared NotAuthenticated, AccessDenied, NoOrganization components + error-card CSS in AdminLayout.css; standardized all 6 admin pages (was: 4 different patterns including inline styles and bare divs). (4) Added order lifecycle state machine docs to schema.prisma and payment status docs to payments.ts. Types pass clean.

title: Get bearings for Migrate dark mode test page CSS to semantic tokens
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Convert dark-mode-test.css from hardcoded hex values to semantic tokens. Update DarkModeTestUI.tsx if it has inline styles with old tokens.

Test Steps:
1. Visit /dark-mode-test page
2. Toggle dark mode - all test swatches should adapt via tokens
3. Compare against expected token values

## STEP 1: Health Check (MANDATORY)

Before exploring for this task, verify the app is healthy:

1. **Start dev server** (if not running) and verify it starts successfully
2. **Run test suite** - Execute existing tests (check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands)
3. **Smoke test via dev-browser** - Navigate to the app and verify a core flow works
   - Use the /dev-browser skill
   - Pick a completed feature from recent work
   - Verify it still functions correctly

### If Health Check FAILS

If ANY of these fail, you CANNOT proceed with this task. Report back to orchestrator:

1. **Identify the cause** - Check git log for recent changes:
   ```bash
   git log --oneline -5
   ```
   Commits should reference bead IDs (e.g., "Refs: beads-xxx")

2. **Document findings** - Add a comment to THIS bead with your investigation

3. **Report to orchestrator** - Do NOT create bug beads or modify epic status yourself.
   The orchestrator will handle creating bug beads and blocking.

## STEP 2: Task-Specific Exploration

Once health check passes:

1. **Search for relevant code** - Use Grep/Glob to find related files
2. **Read key files** - Understand existing patterns and conventions
3. **Identify change locations** - Where will modifications be needed?
4. **Note dependencies** - What other code might be affected?
5. **Document findings** - Add a comment summarizing what you learned

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with your findings, then report to orchestrator.

**If health checks passed**:
```
STATUS: HEALTHY
FILES: <key files that will be modified>
PATTERNS: <existing patterns to follow>
APPROACH: <recommended implementation approach>
CONCERNS: <any concerns or questions>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to implement.
```

**If health checks failed**:
```
STATUS: HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED
FAILURE: <what failed - dev server, tests, smoke test>
ERROR: <error message or description>
CAUSE: <likely cause from git log investigation>
RELATED_EPIC: <bead-id from git log if found, or "unknown">

ACTION: You must handle this health check failure:
1. Check for existing bug beads: `bd list --type=bug --status=open`
2. If no existing bug covers this, create one:
   bd --no-daemon mol pour bug-fix --var title="Health check failure: <brief description>" --var task="<details from my report above>" --var related_epic="<RELATED_EPIC value>" --assignee ralph
3. If RELATED_EPIC is a bead ID, link bug to it: `bd dep add <bug-id> <RELATED_EPIC>`
4. Block current epic on the bug: `bd dep add <your-id> <bug-id>`
5. Move current epic back to open: `bd update <your-id> --status open`
6. Close this bearings step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
7. Exit - do not continue with other steps
```

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work that's clearly needed (validation, security, integration glue, missing API connections):
```bash
bd comments add <root-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description with file context>"
```

## Capturing Learnings
If you encountered something noteworthy:
- An error you struggled with and how you fixed it
- A pattern you had to follow that wasn't documented
- A library/component usage gotcha future agents should know
- Something that might warrant a skill (e.g., "always use X when doing Y")

```bash
bd comments add <root-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"
```

Only add gaps/learnings for non-obvious issues that would help future agents.

comments.author: ptre
comments.text:
STATUS: HEALTHY
FILES: src/app/pages/dark-mode-test/dark-mode-test.css, src/app/pages/dark-mode-test/DarkModeTestUI.tsx
PATTERNS: Semantic tokens defined in tokens.css (--color-text-primary, --color-surface-primary, --color-border-subtle, etc). Dark mode overrides in @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) block.
APPROACH: 1) CSS file: replace hardcoded hex values with semantic tokens where available. Some test-specific values (unique gradients, glows) have no semantic equivalent - these are intentional design experiments and should stay as-is. 2) TSX file: replace old flat tokens (--deep-navy, --cool-gray, --light-gray) with semantic aliases (--color-text-primary, --color-text-secondary, --color-surface-secondary). Also replace hardcoded rgba border with --color-border-subtle.
CONCERNS: Pre-existing TS errors in JoinUI.tsx (autoFocus prop) - unrelated to this task.
ACTION: Close this step and proceed to implement.

title: Diff review UI + prominent date picker
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Diff review UI + prominent date picker

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Build the visual diff display for market update reviews and ensure date handling is robust across all popup forms.

In CommandReview.tsx update_market form:
- Diff layout: two-column or stacked showing field name, old value (struck through / dimmed), new value (highlighted)
- Use var(--color-status-success) background tint for changed fields
- Unchanged fields: collapsed below a "Show all fields" toggle, dimmed text
- If user clicks an unchanged field, it becomes editable (promoted to changed)

Date picker for popup dates:
- Use native <input type="date"> for max compatibility (iOS Safari, Chrome, Firefox)
- Prominently placed — not collapsed or hidden. For create_popup: full-width below schedule field
- Pre-populated with LLM's best guess date
- Label: "Popup expires end of day on:"
- Validation: date must be today or future
- For update_market on popups: date picker always visible even if date didn't change

Time display:
- If LLM extracts a time ("4-7pm"), show it in schedule field
- expiresAt always set to 23:59:59 of the selected date (end of day)

## Test Steps
1. Diff view: changed field shows old→new with visual distinction
2. Unchanged fields dimmed and collapsed
3. Click unchanged field → becomes editable
4. Date picker renders as full-width native input
5. Date pre-populated from LLM response
6. Past dates rejected with validation message
7. expiresAt set to end of selected day (23:59:59)
8. Date picker visible on popup update even if date unchanged
9. Mobile: date picker triggers native date selector
10. Time in schedule field preserved separately from date

title: Verify TipSelector component
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create src/app/pages/orders/components/TipSelector.tsx ('use client'). Props: onTipChange(tipCents), currentTip. Preset buttons: No Tip, $5, $10, Custom. Custom shows input for dollar amount, converts to cents. Selected button highlighted. Tip NOT in platform fee. Design tokens. Compact horizontal layout.

## Test Steps
1. Preset buttons render correctly
2. Clicking $5 calls onTipChange(500)
3. No Tip calls onTipChange(0)
4. Custom shows input field
5. Custom converts dollars to cents
6. Selected button highlighted
7. One option active at a time
8. Design tokens used

## Automated Checks

# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Task Context

3. Refresh URL generates new onboarding link

title: Get bearings for Past popups archive page
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Create dedicated archive page showing completed popup history at /markets/past.

Create src/app/pages/markets/ directory:

PastPopupsPage.tsx (server component):
- Public page (no admin required), uses org context from URL param or session
- Fetch expired/ended popups:
  where: { organizationId, type: "popup", cancelledAt: null,
    OR: [{ active: false }, { expiresAt: { lt: now } }] }
  orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" }
- Cancelled popups excluded (cancelledAt: null filter) — they never happened
- Serialize dates for client

PastPopupsUI.tsx (client component):
- Reverse chronological feed of past popup cards
- Each card shows: name, date (formatted), location, catch preview items (what was available)
- Card styling: muted version of PopupCard (no coral gradient, use var(--color-surface-secondary))
- Read-only — no edit/delete actions
- If >10 popups, group by month ("March 2026", "February 2026")
- Empty state: "No past popups yet" with subtle text, maybe a fish emoji

Route registration:
- Add route /markets/past in customer routes
- Register in worker.tsx under CustomerLayout (same layout as home page)
- Accessible without login (public)

Navigation:
- "See past popups →" link from customer home (already added in previous task)
- Could add to BottomNavigation later (not in this task)

## Test Steps
1. Navigate to /markets/past — shows expired popups newest first
2. Each card shows name, date, location, catch preview
3. Cancelled popups NOT shown
4. Ended-early popups shown (cancelledAt null, active false)
5. Naturally expired popups shown (expiresAt in past)
6. Cards are muted/read-only, no action buttons
7. Empty state renders nicely
8. Month grouping appears when >10 popups
9. Uses CustomerLayout (header, footer)
10. Link from home page works

# ORCHESTRATOR: Order model: add pricing and Stripe fields

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task

(cents, replaces String?), tipAmount Int @default(0), platformFeeBps Int?, platformFee Int?, totalDue Int?, amountPaid Int @default(0), depositAmount Int?, stripeCheckoutSessionId String?, stripePaymentIntentId String?. Keep existing paymentMethod, paymentNotes, paidAt fields.

1. Schema validates (no syntax errors)
2. All new fields have correct types and defaults
3. Existing fields preserved

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description

5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Implement Extend market server functions for popups
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Update src/app/pages/admin/market-functions.ts to handle popup-specific fields, lifecycle actions, and catch preview for both market types.

Changes to existing functions:
- Update FIELD_LIMITS: add catchPreview (2000), notes (1000), rawTranscript (5000)
- Update createMarket() params: accept type, expiresAt, catchPreview, notes, rawTranscript
- Update updateMarket() params: accept same new fields with conditional spreads pattern
- Update validateMarketFields() for new field limits

New functions:
- cancelPopup(id): Sets cancelledAt to now(), active to false. Keeps record for admin history but hidden from customer archive.
- endPopup(id): Sets active to false. cancelledAt stays null (it happened, just ended early).
- updateMarketCatchPreview(id, catchPreview, rawTranscript): Updates just catch preview fields on existing market. Used by update_market_catch intent.

New helpers:
- isPopupExpired(market): market.type === "popup" && market.expiresAt && market.expiresAt < now
- isPopupCancelled(market): market.cancelledAt !== null

All functions follow existing pattern: "use server", requestInfo for context, hasAdminAccess guard, organization scoping.

## Test Steps
1. createMarket with type="popup", expiresAt, catchPreview → saved correctly
2. createMarket with no new fields → type defaults to "regular", others null
3. updateMarket with new fields → conditional spreads work
4. cancelPopup → cancelledAt set, active=false
5. endPopup → active=false, cancelledAt stays null
6. updateMarketCatchPreview → only catchPreview + rawTranscript updated
7. Field validation: catchPreview over 2000 chars rejected
8. All functions require admin access (throw on non-admin)

title: CommandReview + catch/market/popup forms
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: CommandReview + catch/market/popup forms

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Create the polymorphic review component that renders different forms based on the voice command intent.

Create src/components/CommandReview.tsx ("use client"):
- Props: { intent, data, interpretation, rawTranscript, onSave, onCancel, onRetry }
- Header: shows interpretation text ("Updating catch preview for Folly Beach Market")
- Renders as full-screen overlay (half-sheet transitions to full for review space)

Intent-specific forms (switch on intent/reviewType):
- update_catch (reviewType: "catch"): headline input, items list with add/remove (each item: name + note), summary textarea. Reuse CatchUI review form pattern.
- create_market (reviewType: "market-create"): name input, schedule input, locationDetails textarea, customerInfo textarea, catchPreview items editor, active toggle
- create_popup (reviewType: "popup-create"): same as market-create PLUS: expiresAt date picker (PROMINENT — large, not hidden), type shown as "Popup" badge, notes field if populated

All forms:
- Pre-populated with data from voice command response
- All fields editable before save
- Save button, Cancel button, Retry button (re-record)
- Uses design system tokens for styling

## Test Steps
1. update_catch intent → headline + items + summary form renders
2. create_market intent → market creation form renders with all fields
3. create_popup intent → popup form with prominent date picker renders
4. Fields pre-populated from voice command data
5. Edit any field → changes reflected
6. Add/remove catch preview items
7. Save button enabled when required fields present
8. Cancel returns to command bar / closes overlay
9. Retry triggers re-record flow
10. Full-screen overlay renders correctly on mobile

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

title: Get bearings for Voice tool registry + buildCommandPrompt
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Create the voice tool registry that defines all voice actions and generates LLM system prompts. This is the architectural seam: Phase 1 generates prompts, Phase 3 becomes Agents SDK tool definitions.

Create src/api/voice-tools.ts:
- Export voiceTools registry object with these tools:
  update_catch: { description: "Update today's fresh catch with what's available", schema: { headline, items[], summary }, reviewType: "catch" }
  create_market: { description: "Add a new recurring market", schema: { name, schedule, locationDetails?, customerInfo?, catchPreview? }, reviewType: "market-create" }
  create_popup: { description: "Create a temporary popup market event", schema: { name, schedule, expiresAt, locationDetails?, customerInfo?, catchPreview? }, reviewType: "popup-create" }
  update_market: { description: "Update details on an existing market (name match)", schema: { marketId, ...partial fields }, reviewType: "market-update" }
  update_market_catch: { description: "Update what fish are available at a specific market", schema: { marketId, catchPreview }, reviewType: "market-catch" }

- Export function buildCommandPrompt(tools, businessContext):
  - Generates system prompt from registry descriptions + schemas
  - Injects current date (new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]) for relative date resolution
  - Injects business context: list of existing markets (name, id, type, schedule, active)
  - Instructs LLM: return { intent, confidence, data, interpretation }
  - interpretation = human-readable summary ("Update catch preview for Folly Beach Market")
  - If sounds temporary/one-time → type is popup, resolve relative dates to absolute
  - Role-based filtering: accept role param, filter tools by role (admin sees all)

- Export type VoiceCommandResult = { intent: string, confidence: number, data: Record<string, unknown>, interpretation: string }

## Test Steps
1. buildCommandPrompt generates valid prompt containing all tool descriptions
2. Business context (markets list) appears in generated prompt
3. Current date injected correctly
4. Schema shapes described clearly in prompt for LLM
5. Role filtering: non-admin role gets filtered tool list
6. VoiceCommandResult type exported and usable
7. Registry is extensible (adding a new tool is just adding an entry)

title: Verify Market context injection for voice updates
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Enhance the /api/voice/command endpoint to provide richer context when updating existing markets, enabling partial-change detection.

In src/api/voice-command.ts:
- When the LLM returns intent update_market or update_market_catch with a marketId:
  - Fetch full market from DB (all fields including catchPreview)
  - Option A (simpler): The initial prompt already includes all markets with their current state, so the LLM can compute the diff in one pass. Ensure buildCommandPrompt includes enough detail per market (name, schedule, type, active, subtitle, catchPreview summary)
  - Option B (two-pass, more accurate): After first pass identifies intent + target market, re-prompt with full market state asking for only changed fields. This is more token-expensive but more reliable.
  - Start with Option A. If field extraction quality is poor, upgrade to Option B.

- Market name matching: LLM returns marketId from the injected market list. If the market list includes IDs, the LLM can return the exact ID. If fuzzy matching needed, compare LLM-returned name against market names using simple substring/lowercase match.

- For update_market_catch: merge new catch preview with any existing catch preview if needed, or full replace

- Accept optional query param ?marketId=xxx for explicit targeting (e.g., from admin UI "update this market" button)

## Test Steps
1. "Folly Beach moved to Sundays" → marketId correctly matched, only schedule in changed data
2. "Market's paused until April" → active=false and subtitle in changed data
3. "Got shrimp for Folly Beach" → update_market_catch with correct marketId
4. Explicit ?marketId param → uses that market regardless of voice content
5. Market not found → graceful error
6. Ambiguous name (multiple partial matches) → returns lower confidence
7. Full market state available in prompt context

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)

title: Verify Data migration for existing orders
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal

## Task Context
Create data migration script: parse existing string price values to cents where possible (e.g. '45' -> 4500, '$45.50' -> 4550). Set unparseable prices to null. Set amountPaid based on old paymentStatus (if was 'paid', set amountPaid = price). Handle null prices. Log results.

## Test Steps
1. Numeric string prices converted correctly
2. Dollar sign prices converted
3. Ranges/text prices set to null

title: Get bearings for Wire CommandBar to voice/command endpoint
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Connect the CommandBar FAB to the /api/voice/command endpoint so recording/typing produces a VoiceCommandResult that gets passed to the review layer.

In CommandBar.tsx:
- On audio ready (from useVoiceRecorder onAudioReady callback): POST blob as ArrayBuffer to /api/voice/command with Content-Type of the audio MIME type
- On text submit: POST JSON { text } to /api/voice/command with Content-Type application/json
- Parse response: extract { intent, confidence, data, interpretation, rawTranscript, reviewType }
- Call onResult(result) prop with the parsed response
- Error handling: show error state in half-sheet, retain blob for retry
- Loading state: show processing indicator while waiting for response

In AdminLayout (or parent):
- Receive onResult from CommandBar
- Store result in state
- When result present, render CommandReview component (built in next task) as overlay
- On review save/cancel, clear result state

## Test Steps
1. Record audio in command bar → POST sent to /api/voice/command
2. Response parsed and onResult called with VoiceCommandResult
3. Text mode: type and submit → same endpoint called with JSON
4. Error from endpoint → error state shown in half-sheet
5. Retry after error → blob reused, no re-recording needed
6. Processing indicator visible during API call
7. Result passed to parent triggers review overlay
8. Half-sheet closes when review opens

## STEP 1: Health Check (MANDATORY)

Before exploring for this task, verify the app is healthy:

1. **Start dev server** (if not running) and verify it starts successfully
2. **Run test suite** - Execute existing tests (check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands)

   - Verify it still functions correctly

description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Implement: charge.refunded -> create Payment(amount negative, method=stripe, type=refund), decrement amountPaid, clear paidAt if now under totalDue. account.updated -> find org by stripeAccountId, check charges_enabled AND details_submitted, update stripeOnboardingComplete.

## Test Steps
1. charge.refunded creates negative Payment record
2. amountPaid decremented correctly
3. Full refund resets paidAt
4. account.updated sets onboarding complete when ready
5. account.updated keeps incomplete when not ready
6. Unknown account ID handled gracefully

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)

title: Verify Review: update diff, confidence, save wiring
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Add market update diff view, low-confidence warning, and wire all review forms to their server functions.

In CommandReview.tsx, add:

update_market (reviewType: "market-update") form:
- Diff view: changed fields highlighted (old→new values shown), unchanged fields dimmed below
- User can toggle unchanged fields to edit them too
- Date picker always visible for popup expiresAt changes

update_market_catch (reviewType: "market-catch") form:
- Market name shown read-only (matched by voice)
- catchPreview items editable (add/remove, name+note per item)

Confidence warning (all intents):
- If confidence < 0.7: show yellow warning banner: "I'm not sure about this. Did you mean: [interpretation]?"
- "That's not right" button → options: re-record, or manual intent selection dropdown
- Manual intent selection: dropdown of all tool descriptions, selecting one re-renders the appropriate form with existing data

Save wiring (all intents):
- Save button calls the appropriate server function based on intent:
  update_catch → publishCatch(content, rawTranscript) from catch-functions.ts
  create_market → createMarket(data) from market-functions.ts
  create_popup → createMarket({...data, type: "popup"}) from market-functions.ts
  update_market → updateMarket(marketId, changedFields) from market-functions.ts
  update_market_catch → updateMarketCatchPreview(marketId, catchPreview, rawTranscript) from market-functions.ts
- On save success: close overlay, show brief success toast, trigger page refresh/revalidation
- On save error: show error in review, don't close

## Test Steps
1. update_market intent → diff view shows changed vs unchanged fields
2. Changed fields highlighted, old→new values visible
3. update_market_catch intent → market name read-only, items editable
4. Low confidence (<0.7) → warning banner shown
5. "That's not right" → re-record option works
6. Manual intent selection dropdown appears and re-renders form
7. Save update_catch → publishCatch called, success toast
8. Save create_popup → createMarket called with type="popup"
9. Save update_market → updateMarket called with only changed fields
10. Save error → error shown, overlay stays open
11. Success → overlay closes, page refreshes

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description

4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)

title: Commit Market context injection for voice updates
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Enhance the /api/voice/command endpoint to provide richer context when updating existing markets, enabling partial-change detection.

In src/api/voice-command.ts:
- When the LLM returns intent update_market or update_market_catch with a marketId:
  - Fetch full market from DB (all fields including catchPreview)
  - Option A (simpler): The initial prompt already includes all markets with their current state, so the LLM can compute the diff in one pass. Ensure buildCommandPrompt includes enough detail per market (name, schedule, type, active, subtitle, catchPreview summary)
  - Option B (two-pass, more accurate): After first pass identifies intent + target market, re-prompt with full market state asking for only changed fields. This is more token-expensive but more reliable.
  - Start with Option A. If field extraction quality is poor, upgrade to Option B.

- Market name matching: LLM returns marketId from the injected market list. If the market list includes IDs, the LLM can return the exact ID. If fuzzy matching needed, compare LLM-returned name against market names using simple substring/lowercase match.

- For update_market_catch: merge new catch preview with any existing catch preview if needed, or full replace

- Accept optional query param ?marketId=xxx for explicit targeting (e.g., from admin UI "update this market" button)

## Test Steps
1. "Folly Beach moved to Sundays" → marketId correctly matched, only schedule in changed data
2. "Market's paused until April" → active=false and subtitle in changed data
3. "Got shrimp for Folly Beach" → update_market_catch with correct marketId
4. Explicit ?marketId param → uses that market regardless of voice content
5. Market not found → graceful error
6. Ambiguous name (multiple partial matches) → returns lower confidence
7. Full market state available in prompt context

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

## Commit Message Format
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

## Output to Orchestrator

- ensureDb() called since webhook runs before middleware
- Platform metadata validation (fresh-catch)

title: Customer home popup integration
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Customer home popup integration

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Integrate popup cards into customer home page and add catch preview support to regular market cards.

CustomerHome.tsx (server component):
- Fetch active popups separately:
  where: { organizationId, type: "popup", active: true, cancelledAt: null }
  orderBy: { expiresAt: "asc" } (soonest first)
- Filter in code: expiresAt is null OR expiresAt >= start of today
- Serialize for client: convert dates to ISO strings
- Pass popups array to CustomerHomeUI

CustomerHomeUI.tsx (client component):
- New section above regular markets: "Coming Up" or "Popup Events"
- Only rendered if popups array is non-empty
- Render PopupCard for each active popup
- Multiple popups on same day: stack as separate cards

Regular MarketCard enhancement:
- If market has catchPreview (JSON string), parse and display
- Subtle treatment: below schedule, small text: "Usually available: Mahi, Grouper, Shrimp"
- Just item names, no notes (keep compact for regular cards)
- Only show if catchPreview has items

Small link below popup section: "See past popups →" → /markets/past (even if archive page not built yet, link is ready)

## Test Steps
1. Active popup appears in "Coming Up" section above markets
2. Expired popup not shown (expiresAt < today filtered out)
3. Cancelled popup not shown (cancelledAt filter)
4. Multiple popups render as stacked cards

title: Past popups archive page
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Past popups archive page

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Create dedicated archive page showing completed popup history at /markets/past.

Create src/app/pages/markets/ directory:

PastPopupsPage.tsx (server component):
- Public page (no admin required), uses org context from URL param or session
- Fetch expired/ended popups:
  where: { organizationId, type: "popup", cancelledAt: null,
    OR: [{ active: false }, { expiresAt: { lt: now } }] }
  orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" }
- Cancelled popups excluded (cancelledAt: null filter) — they never happened
- Serialize dates for client

PastPopupsUI.tsx (client component):
- Reverse chronological feed of past popup cards
- Each card shows: name, date (formatted), location, catch preview items (what was available)
- Card styling: muted version of PopupCard (no coral gradient, use var(--color-surface-secondary))
- Read-only — no edit/delete actions
- If >10 popups, group by month ("March 2026", "February 2026")
- Empty state: "No past popups yet" with subtle text, maybe a fish emoji

Route registration:
- Add route /markets/past in customer routes
- Register in worker.tsx under CustomerLayout (same layout as home page)
- Accessible without login (public)

Navigation:
- "See past popups →" link from customer home (already added in previous task)
- Could add to BottomNavigation later (not in this task)

## Test Steps
1. Navigate to /markets/past — shows expired popups newest first
2. Each card shows name, date, location, catch preview
3. Cancelled popups NOT shown
4. Ended-early popups shown (cancelledAt null, active false)
5. Naturally expired popups shown (expiresAt in past)
6. Cards are muted/read-only, no action buttons

8. Month grouping appears when >10 popups

description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
The utility classes in tokens.css reference old flat names. Update them to use semantic names. Also update placeholder styling rule and button hover styles.

Test Steps:
1. Run pnpm run dev
2. Check pages using utility classes - headings, body text, captions render correctly
3. Toggle dark mode - utility classes adapt
4. Grep tokens.css for remaining old flat names outside :root

## STEP 1: Health Check (MANDATORY)

Before exploring for this task, verify the app is healthy:

1. **Start dev server** (if not running) and verify it starts successfully
2. **Run test suite** - Execute existing tests (check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands)
3. **Smoke test via dev-browser** - Navigate to the app and verify a core flow works
   - Use the /dev-browser skill
   - Pick a completed feature from recent work
   - Verify it still functions correctly

### If Health Check FAILS

If ANY of these fail, you CANNOT proceed with this task. Report back to orchestrator:

1. **Identify the cause** - Check git log for recent changes:
   ```bash
   git log --oneline -5
   ```
   Commits should reference bead IDs (e.g., "Refs: beads-xxx")

2. **Document findings** - Add a comment to THIS bead with your investigation

3. **Report to orchestrator** - Do NOT create bug beads or modify epic status yourself.
   The orchestrator will handle creating bug beads and blocking.

## STEP 2: Task-Specific Exploration

Once health check passes:

1. **Search for relevant code** - Use Grep/Glob to find related files
2. **Read key files** - Understand existing patterns and conventions
3. **Identify change locations** - Where will modifications be needed?
4. **Note dependencies** - What other code might be affected?
5. **Document findings** - Add a comment summarizing what you learned

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with your findings, then report to orchestrator.

**If health checks passed**:
```
STATUS: HEALTHY
FILES: <key files that will be modified>
PATTERNS: <existing patterns to follow>
APPROACH: <recommended implementation approach>
CONCERNS: <any concerns or questions>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to implement.
```

**If health checks failed**:
```
STATUS: HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED
FAILURE: <what failed - dev server, tests, smoke test>
ERROR: <error message or description>
CAUSE: <likely cause from git log investigation>
RELATED_EPIC: <bead-id from git log if found, or "unknown">

ACTION: You must handle this health check failure:
1. Check for existing bug beads: `bd list --type=bug --status=open`
2. If no existing bug covers this, create one:
   bd --no-daemon mol pour bug-fix --var title="Health check failure: <brief description>" --var task="<details from my report above>" --var related_epic="<RELATED_EPIC value>" --assignee ralph
3. If RELATED_EPIC is a bead ID, link bug to it: `bd dep add <bug-id> <RELATED_EPIC>`
4. Block current epic on the bug: `bd dep add <your-id> <bug-id>`
5. Move current epic back to open: `bd update <your-id> --status open`
6. Close this bearings step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
7. Exit - do not continue with other steps
```

  --warm-white → --color-surface-warm
  --text-primary → --color-text-primary (intermediate alias, still flat)
  --text-secondary → --color-text-secondary

3 areas to update:
1. Typography utility classes (lines 492-514): .heading-*, .body-*, .label-*, .caption, .text-primary, .text-secondary — replace --deep-navy/--cool-gray with --color-text-primary/--color-text-secondary
2. Component utility classes (lines 560-608): .status-badge, .info-box variants — replace --light-gray/--deep-navy/--warm-gold/--mint-fresh/--coral with semantic aliases
3. Additional typography utils (lines 651-678): .text-heading-lg, .text-subheading, .text-order-number, .text-metadata — replace --text-primary/--text-secondary/--ocean-blue with semantic names
4. Placeholder styling (line 398): --cool-gray → --color-text-secondary
5. Button hover styles (lines 267-281): hardcoded rgba values + --ocean-blue → semantic tokens
6. Dark mode placeholder (line 482): --cool-gray → --color-text-secondary

APPROACH:
Pure find-and-replace in tokens.css utility section (lines 487+), placeholder rules (398, 482), and button hover rules (267-281). No structural changes. All semantic tokens already exist in :root. Zero risk of breaking consumers since we're changing CSS var references inside class definitions, not renaming the classes themselves.

CONCERNS:
- Button hover rgba(0,102,204,...) hardcoded values (lines 268, 272, 277, 281) have no direct semantic token equivalent. Could leave as-is or extract new tokens. Recommend leaving hardcoded rgba hover shadows for now, only updating --ocean-blue ref on line 278.
- --text-primary/--text-secondary (lines 157-158) are intermediate aliases already pointing to --deep-navy/--cool-gray. Lines 655/662/676 use these. Should migrate to --color-text-primary/--color-text-secondary.

description:

## Task

title: Verify Voice tool registry + buildCommandPrompt
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create the voice tool registry that defines all voice actions and generates LLM system prompts. This is the architectural seam: Phase 1 generates prompts, Phase 3 becomes Agents SDK tool definitions.

Create src/api/voice-tools.ts:
- Export voiceTools registry object with these tools:
  update_catch: { description: "Update today's fresh catch with what's available", schema: { headline, items[], summary }, reviewType: "catch" }
  create_market: { description: "Add a new recurring market", schema: { name, schedule, locationDetails?, customerInfo?, catchPreview? }, reviewType: "market-create" }
  create_popup: { description: "Create a temporary popup market event", schema: { name, schedule, expiresAt, locationDetails?, customerInfo?, catchPreview? }, reviewType: "popup-create" }
  update_market: { description: "Update details on an existing market (name match)", schema: { marketId, ...partial fields }, reviewType: "market-update" }
  update_market_catch: { description: "Update what fish are available at a specific market", schema: { marketId, catchPreview }, reviewType: "market-catch" }

- Export function buildCommandPrompt(tools, businessContext):
  - Generates system prompt from registry descriptions + schemas
  - Injects current date (new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]) for relative date resolution
  - Injects business context: list of existing markets (name, id, type, schedule, active)
  - Instructs LLM: return { intent, confidence, data, interpretation }
  - interpretation = human-readable summary ("Update catch preview for Folly Beach Market")
  - If sounds temporary/one-time → type is popup, resolve relative dates to absolute
  - Role-based filtering: accept role param, filter tools by role (admin sees all)

- Export type VoiceCommandResult = { intent: string, confidence: number, data: Record<string, unknown>, interpretation: string }

## Test Steps
1. buildCommandPrompt generates valid prompt containing all tool descriptions
2. Business context (markets list) appears in generated prompt
3. Current date injected correctly
4. Schema shapes described clearly in prompt for LLM
5. Role filtering: non-admin role gets filtered tool list
6. VoiceCommandResult type exported and usable
7. Registry is extensible (adding a new tool is just adding an entry)

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with verification results, then report to orchestrator.

**If all checks pass**:
```
STATUS: PASS
TYPES: pass
TESTS: pass
LINT: pass (or N/A)
UI: pass (or N/A)
SCREENSHOTS: <paths if applicable>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to commit.
```

**If checks fail (significant issues)**:
```
STATUS: FAIL
TYPES: pass/fail
TESTS: pass/fail
LINT: pass/fail
UI: pass/fail
ISSUE: <what's wrong>
SUGGESTION: <what implement should try differently>

ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
1. Count `[attempt-N]` comments on your bead: `bd comments <your-id>`
2. If < 3 attempts:
   - Add attempt comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[attempt-N] Implementation attempt N: FAIL - <ISSUE from above>"`
   - Reopen implement step: `bd update <implement-step-id> --status open`
   - Add guidance: `bd comments add <implement-step-id> "[REWORK] <SUGGESTION from above>"`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Wait for implement to complete, then verify again
3. If >= 3 attempts:
   - Add critical comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[CRITICAL] Implementation failed after 3 attempts. Requires human review."`
   - Block yourself: `bd update <your-id> --status blocked`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Exit
```

## Capturing Gaps

title: Admin Catch Recording UI
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Admin Catch Recording UI

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

## Overview
Build the admin catch recording page (/admin/catch) with a massive dock-friendly mic button, integrate with the audio processing API (built in a prior task), add the dashboard nav card, and register the route. This is the full admin-side UI for voice-to-catch.

## Part 1: Admin Catch Page (Server Component)

### CatchPage.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchPage.tsx)
- Server component following existing pattern (see MarketConfigPage.tsx as reference)
- Auth check: hasAdminAccess(ctx), return appropriate error components if not
- Fetch latest live CatchUpdate for org: `db.catchUpdate.findFirst({ where: { organizationId: ctx.currentOrganization.id, status: "live" }, orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" } })`
- Pass currentCatch (or null) + ctx to CatchUI client component

## Part 2: Admin Catch UI (Client Component)

### CatchUI.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchUI.tsx)
- "use client" component
- State machine with states: idle | recording | processing | done | error | permission-denied

### Mic Button (centerpiece)
- 120px+ diameter circle, `border-radius: var(--radius-full)`
- Idle: ocean blue (`var(--color-action-primary)`), mic icon (SVG or emoji)
- Text below: "Tap to record what's fresh"
- Must be easy to tap with a big thumb on a dock

### Recording State
- Pulsing red ring animation (CSS keyframes)
- 2-minute countdown timer display (mm:ss format)
- Audio level indicator: use AudioContext + AnalyserNode
  - Connect MediaStream source to AnalyserNode
  - Read frequency data in requestAnimationFrame loop
  - Map amplitude to visual feedback (ring glow intensity or thickness pulsing with voice)
  - Evan needs to SEE the mic is picking up sound over dock wind/noise
- Auto-stop AND auto-submit at 0:00 (same behavior as manual stop)
- Text: "Recording... Tap to stop"

### MediaRecorder Integration
- MIME type detection: `MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported()`
  - Prefer `audio/webm;codecs=opus` (Chrome, Android)
  - Fallback: `audio/mp4` (Safari, iOS)
  - Fallback: `audio/webm`
- getUserMedia: `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: { echoCancellation: true, noiseSuppression: true } })`
- On stop: convert blob to ArrayBuffer, POST to /api/catch/record
- Release mic tracks on stop: `stream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())`

### Error State (CRITICAL for dock use)
- RETAIN audio blob in memory on upload failure
- "Retry" button: re-uploads the SAME recording (no re-recording needed)
- "Re-record" button: separate, discards blob, returns to idle
- Error message displayed clearly
- Critical for spotty dock signal — don't lose a 2-minute recording on failure

### Permission Denied State
- Handle navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia rejection
- Show explanation: "Microphone access is needed to record your catch update"
- Instructions to re-enable in browser settings
- Retry button to request permission again

### Processing State
- Spinner animation
- Text: "Transcribing & formatting..."
- Non-interactive (no cancel, let it complete)

### Done State
- Green checkmark or success indicator
- Show formatted catch result (headline, item list, summary)
- "Re-record" button to start over
- "Clear catch" button to archive current live catch without replacing

### Current Live Catch Display
- Below the mic button area
- "Currently showing to customers:" header
- Render the current live catch data if exists
- Show "No catch currently displayed" if none

### Clear Catch
- Button that archives current live catch without recording new one
- Calls clearCatch() server function
- Use case: off-season, or Evan wants to stop showing catch

## Part 3: Server Functions (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch-functions.ts)
- "use server" module
- `clearCatch()`: archives current live catch for org, calls revalidatePath("/")
- Follow existing server function patterns: requestInfo, hasAdminAccess check, return { success, error? }

## Part 4: Styles (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch.css)
- BEM-like naming: `.catch-mic`, `.catch-mic__ring`, `.catch-mic__ring--recording`, `.catch-mic__timer`, `.catch-result`, etc.
- Use semantic design tokens ONLY (var(--color-*), var(--space-*), var(--radius-*))
- @keyframes for pulse animation (recording state)
- @keyframes for amplitude visualization
- Mobile-first: big touch targets, readable on small screens
- @media (max-width: 640px) adjustments if needed

## Part 5: Dashboard Nav Card + Route Registration

### AdminDashboardUI.tsx
- Add 4th nav card to the existing grid:
  - Icon: 🎙️ (microphone emoji)
  - Title: "Fresh Catch"
  - Description: "Record what's available this week"
  - Link: /admin/catch

### routes.ts (src/app/pages/admin/routes.ts)
- Import CatchPage from ./catch/CatchPage
- Add: route("/catch", CatchPage)

## Test Steps
1. Navigate to /admin/catch as admin user — page loads with mic button
2. See big mic button in idle state (120px+, ocean blue)
3. Tap mic — browser requests microphone permission
4. Grant permission — see recording state: red pulsing ring, countdown from 2:00
5. See audio level indicator responding to voice input (visual amplitude)
6. Tap again to stop — see processing spinner ("Transcribing & formatting...")
7. After processing: see formatted catch result with headline, items, summary
8. See "Re-record" button in done state
9. Wait 2 minutes while recording — auto-stops and auto-submits
10. Simulate upload failure: see error with both "Retry" and "Re-record" buttons
11. Tap "Retry" — re-uploads same audio without re-recording
12. Test "Clear catch" — archives current catch
13. Deny mic permission — see helpful permission-denied message with instructions
14. Navigate to /admin — see 4th "Fresh Catch" card with mic icon
15. Click "Fresh Catch" card — navigates to /admin/catch
16. Test on mobile viewport: mic button easy to tap with thumb
17. Verify all styles use semantic design tokens (no hardcoded hex)

## Context Window Management
If approaching 30-40% context usage with remaining work, create continuation beads for remaining items and exit cleanly.

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

title: useVoiceRecorder hook + VoiceMicButton component
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: useVoiceRecorder hook + VoiceMicButton component

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** infrastructure

Extract shared voice recording infrastructure into reusable modules:

Create src/hooks/useVoiceRecorder.ts:
- Encapsulates: MediaRecorder lifecycle, AudioContext + AnalyserNode, amplitude state, timer (configurable duration, default 120s), blob management + retention on error, MIME type detection (webm/mp4), cleanup

- State type: "idle" | "recording" | "processing" | "done" | "error" | "permission-denied"

description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Update src/app/pages/admin/MarketConfigUI.tsx to display popups separately from regular markets with visual distinction and status badges.

Layout changes (three sections replacing current two):
1. Active Popups (shown first if any exist):
   - Distinct visual treatment (coral/warm accent border or background tint)
   - Each popup shows: name, date, time, location summary, catch preview summary (if any)
   - Date badge: "TODAY" / "TOMORROW" / formatted date
   - Expiration countdown or status
2. Active Regular Markets:
   - Existing layout, unchanged
3. Inactive / Expired (collapsed by default):
   - Both types mixed, sorted by updatedAt desc
   - Expired popups: "Expired" badge
   - Cancelled popups: "Cancelled" badge (distinct color from expired)
   - Manually deactivated: "Inactive" badge (existing behavior)

Section headers with counts: "Active Popups (2)" / "Markets (3)" / "Inactive (5)"

- Fetch all markets (not just active), pass full list to UI
- UI handles filtering/grouping client-side

- Add "Add Popup" button next to it (opens form modal with type pre-set to popup)

# ORCHESTRATOR: Migrate remaining page component inline styles

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

2. Run pnpm run dev - full app smoke test
3. Toggle dark mode across all pages
4. Final grep: zero old flat token names remaining

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
   - `ralph-inline-*`: Execute the step yourself following its description
3. **Read what each step reports** and follow its instructions exactly
4. **Close completed steps**: `bd close <step-id>`
5. **Repeat** until no more steps

## Commands
- Check ready children: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
- View step details: `bd show <step-id>`
- Close completed step: `bd close <step-id>`
- Add progress note: `bd comments add <step-id> "..."`

## Following Step Instructions

Each step will report back with:
- A STATUS (e.g., HEALTHY, PASS, FAIL, HEALTH_CHECK_FAILED)
- Instructions for what you should do next

**You must follow the step's instructions.** Steps may tell you to:
- Proceed to the next step
- Reopen a previous step with guidance
- Create a bug bead and block
- Mark yourself as blocked

Do exactly what the step tells you. The step knows its domain.

## Completion

When all steps are done:

1. **Add summary comment**:
   ```bash
   bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] Completed: <brief description>"
   ```

2. **Check for [GAP] comments** on yourself:
   - Run `bd comments <your-id>` and look for `[GAP]` entries
   - If any exist AND no gap-review step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> gaps`

3. **Check for [LEARNING] comments** on yourself:
   - Look for `[LEARNING]` entries in your comments
   - If any exist AND no learning-capture step ran, add label: `bd label add <your-id> learnings`

4. **Close yourself**: `bd close <your-id>`

**IMPORTANT**: Complete this entire molecule (all applicable steps), then exit. Do NOT look for or grab another molecule after. This keeps changes small and commits atomic.

If the molecule cannot complete (blocker, error, etc.), move the root bead back to `open` status before exiting.

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Commit CommandBar FAB component + half-sheet
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Create the floating action button (mic) and half-sheet recording UI that mounts on all admin pages.

Create src/components/CommandBar.tsx ("use client"):
- FAB: fixed position bottom-right (16px from edges on mobile), 56px circle, mic icon, z-index above content, shadow + hover lift
- Tap FAB → half-sheet slides up from bottom (CSS transition, max-height 70vh)
- Half-sheet contains:
  - VoiceMicButton (shared component from useVoiceRecorder)
  - Hint chips: contextual based on optional hintContext prop (catch page → "what's fresh today", markets page → "add market, create popup, update details")
  - Input mode toggle: Voice | Type (same pattern as CatchUI)
  - Text textarea fallback with hint text as placeholder
- Close triggers: Escape key, tap outside half-sheet, swipe down (optional)
- Props: onResult(result: VoiceCommandResult), hintContext?: string

Create src/components/command-bar.css:
- FAB: circular, var(--color-action-primary), var(--shadow-lg)
- Half-sheet: slides up, border-radius top corners (var(--radius-xl)), backdrop blur, var(--color-surface-primary) background
- Slide-up animation (transform translateY)
- Mobile: full-width half-sheet

Mount in AdminLayout (or admin route wrapper):
- Import and render CommandBar
- Wire onResult to state that will be consumed by CommandReview (next task)

title: Implement Review: update diff, confidence, save wiring
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Add market update diff view, low-confidence warning, and wire all review forms to their server functions.

In CommandReview.tsx, add:

update_market (reviewType: "market-update") form:
- Diff view: changed fields highlighted (old→new values shown), unchanged fields dimmed below
- User can toggle unchanged fields to edit them too
- Date picker always visible for popup expiresAt changes

update_market_catch (reviewType: "market-catch") form:
- Market name shown read-only (matched by voice)
- catchPreview items editable (add/remove, name+note per item)

Confidence warning (all intents):
- If confidence < 0.7: show yellow warning banner: "I'm not sure about this. Did you mean: [interpretation]?"
- "That's not right" button → options: re-record, or manual intent selection dropdown
- Manual intent selection: dropdown of all tool descriptions, selecting one re-renders the appropriate form with existing data

Save wiring (all intents):
- Save button calls the appropriate server function based on intent:
  update_catch → publishCatch(content, rawTranscript) from catch-functions.ts
  create_market → createMarket(data) from market-functions.ts
  create_popup → createMarket({...data, type: "popup"}) from market-functions.ts
  update_market → updateMarket(marketId, changedFields) from market-functions.ts
  update_market_catch → updateMarketCatchPreview(marketId, catchPreview, rawTranscript) from market-functions.ts
- On save success: close overlay, show brief success toast, trigger page refresh/revalidation
- On save error: show error in review, don't close

## Test Steps
1. update_market intent → diff view shows changed vs unchanged fields
2. Changed fields highlighted, old→new values visible
3. update_market_catch intent → market name read-only, items editable
4. Low confidence (<0.7) → warning banner shown
5. "That's not right" → re-record option works
6. Manual intent selection dropdown appears and re-renders form
7. Save update_catch → publishCatch called, success toast
8. Save create_popup → createMarket called with type="popup"
9. Save update_market → updateMarket called with only changed fields
10. Save error → error shown, overlay stays open
11. Success → overlay closes, page refreshes

title: Verify Update markAsPaid to create Payment records
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Refactor markAsPaid in order-functions.ts: accept orderId, amount (cents), method, notes. Create Payment record { orderId, amount, method, type: payment, notes }. Increment order.amountPaid. If amountPaid >= totalDue: set paidAt. Keep paymentMethod/paymentNotes on order for compat. Validate: positive amount, confirmed/completed order.

## Test Steps
1. Creates Payment record
2. amountPaid incremented
3. Partial: paidAt not set
4. Full: paidAt set
5. Method stored on Payment
6. Notes stored
7. Rejects negative amount
8. Rejects pending/cancelled order
9. pnpm run types passes

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)

- [ ] Code review looks good

title: Commit Shared voice-pipeline.ts server-side
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Extract shared server-side voice processing pipeline from catch-record.ts into a reusable module.

Create src/api/voice-pipeline.ts:
- Function: processVoiceInput(request, ctx, options: { systemPrompt: string, validate: (obj: unknown) => T })
- Handles auth checks (ctx.user, hasAdminAccess, currentOrganization)
- Content-Type branching: application/json → text input, else → audio binary
- Audio path: Whisper transcription via env.AI.run("@cf/openai/whisper-tiny-en", { audio: [...new Uint8Array(body)] })
- Text path: extract text from JSON body
- LLM formatting: env.AI.run("@cf/meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct-fp8-fast", { messages: [...] }) with provided systemPrompt
- Robust JSON parsing: direct parse → strip markdown fences → validate shape
- Returns: { formatted: T, rawTranscript: string }
- Error handling: 401/403 for auth, 400 for empty input, 500 for transcription/formatting failures

Reference: src/api/catch-record.ts for the existing implementation to extract from.

## Test Steps
1. processVoiceInput with audio Content-Type → Whisper called
2. processVoiceInput with application/json → text extracted directly
3. Auth: returns 401 without user, 403 without admin access
4. Empty audio → 400 error
5. Empty text → 400 error
6. Whisper failure → 500 with rawTranscript if available
7. LLM response with markdown fences → parsed correctly
8. Custom validate function called and rejects invalid shapes
9. Returns { formatted, rawTranscript } on success

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

Refs: <bead-id>

description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Update CustomerOrdersUI.tsx: import PriceBreakdown, TipSelector, PaymentActions. For confirmed orders: show PriceBreakdown, TipSelector (unpaid only), PaymentActions. Pay handler calls createCheckoutSession and redirects. Track tip state per order with useState. Update server component to include pricing fields in query.

2. Unpaid order shows tip selector
3. Unpaid order shows pay button
4. Pay button creates session and redirects
5. Tip state per order independent
6. Paid orders hide tip selector
7. Pending orders no payment UI
8. Server query includes pricing fields

## STEP 1: Health Check (MANDATORY)

## Test Steps

2. Updates feeModel
3. Rejects invalid feeBps
4. Rejects invalid feeModel
5. updateDepositConfig sets depositBps

title: Wire CommandBar to voice/command endpoint
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Wire CommandBar to voice/command endpoint

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Connect the CommandBar FAB to the /api/voice/command endpoint so recording/typing produces a VoiceCommandResult that gets passed to the review layer.

In CommandBar.tsx:
- On audio ready (from useVoiceRecorder onAudioReady callback): POST blob as ArrayBuffer to /api/voice/command with Content-Type of the audio MIME type
- On text submit: POST JSON { text } to /api/voice/command with Content-Type application/json
- Parse response: extract { intent, confidence, data, interpretation, rawTranscript, reviewType }
- Call onResult(result) prop with the parsed response
- Error handling: show error state in half-sheet, retain blob for retry
- Loading state: show processing indicator while waiting for response

In AdminLayout (or parent):
- Receive onResult from CommandBar
- Store result in state
- When result present, render CommandReview component (built in next task) as overlay
- On review save/cancel, clear result state

## Test Steps
1. Record audio in command bar → POST sent to /api/voice/command
2. Response parsed and onResult called with VoiceCommandResult
3. Text mode: type and submit → same endpoint called with JSON
4. Error from endpoint → error state shown in half-sheet
5. Retry after error → blob reused, no re-recording needed
6. Processing indicator visible during API call
7. Result passed to parent triggers review overlay
8. Half-sheet closes when review opens

## Execution Loop

1. **Find ready steps**: `bd ready --parent <your-id>`
2. **For each ready step**, route by assignee prefix:
   - `ralph-subagent-*`: Spawn a Task agent with the step's description (from `bd show <step-id>`)
