title: Commit Review: update diff, confidence, save wiring
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Add market update diff view, low-confidence warning, and wire all review forms to their server functions.

In CommandReview.tsx, add:

update_market (reviewType: "market-update") form:
- Diff view: changed fields highlighted (old→new values shown), unchanged fields dimmed below
- User can toggle unchanged fields to edit them too
- Date picker always visible for popup expiresAt changes

update_market_catch (reviewType: "market-catch") form:
- Market name shown read-only (matched by voice)
- catchPreview items editable (add/remove, name+note per item)

Confidence warning (all intents):
- If confidence < 0.7: show yellow warning banner: "I'm not sure about this. Did you mean: [interpretation]?"
- "That's not right" button → options: re-record, or manual intent selection dropdown
- Manual intent selection: dropdown of all tool descriptions, selecting one re-renders the appropriate form with existing data

Save wiring (all intents):
- Save button calls the appropriate server function based on intent:
  update_catch → publishCatch(content, rawTranscript) from catch-functions.ts
  create_market → createMarket(data) from market-functions.ts
  create_popup → createMarket({...data, type: "popup"}) from market-functions.ts
  update_market → updateMarket(marketId, changedFields) from market-functions.ts
  update_market_catch → updateMarketCatchPreview(marketId, catchPreview, rawTranscript) from market-functions.ts
- On save success: close overlay, show brief success toast, trigger page refresh/revalidation
- On save error: show error in review, don't close

## Test Steps
1. update_market intent → diff view shows changed vs unchanged fields
2. Changed fields highlighted, old→new values visible
3. update_market_catch intent → market name read-only, items editable
4. Low confidence (<0.7) → warning banner shown
5. "That's not right" → re-record option works
6. Manual intent selection dropdown appears and re-renders form
7. Save update_catch → publishCatch called, success toast
8. Save create_popup → createMarket called with type="popup"
9. Save update_market → updateMarket called with only changed fields
10. Save error → error shown, overlay stays open
11. Success → overlay closes, page refreshes

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

## Commit Message Format
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment with the commit details:
```
STATUS: DONE
COMMIT: <commit hash>
MESSAGE: <commit message summary>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to next steps (if any).
```

When complete, close this bead with `bd close <this-bead-id>`.

title: Implement Market popup schema migration
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Add popup and voice fields to Market model in prisma/schema.prisma. New fields (all optional/defaulted for backward compat):
- type String @default("regular") // "regular" | "popup"
- expiresAt DateTime?
- catchPreview String? // JSON: { items: [{name, note}] }
- notes String? // catch-all for unstructured voice content
- rawTranscript String? // verbatim Whisper output (null for form-created)
- cancelledAt DateTime? // set if popup cancelled before happening
- Add index: @@index([organizationId, type, active])
- Create migration: pnpm run migrate:new (name: add-popup-market-fields)
- Apply locally: pnpm run migrate:dev
- Regenerate types: pnpm run generate

## Test Steps
1. pnpm run migrate:dev — no errors
2. pnpm run generate — Prisma client regenerated
3. pnpm run types — no type errors
4. Existing market CRUD still works (all new fields optional/defaulted)
5. Can create a market with type="popup" and expiresAt set

## Instructions

1. **Review bearings notes** - Check the bearings step's comments for context
2. **Make focused changes** - Implement one thing at a time
3. **Follow existing patterns** - Match the codebase style
4. **Keep changes minimal** - Don't refactor unrelated code
5. **Add necessary tests** - If the codebase has tests, add relevant ones

## Best Practices
- Use Edit tool for modifications (not Write for existing files)
- Preserve existing formatting and conventions
- Add comments for complex logic
- Consider edge cases

title: Commit confirmOrder: Stripe Checkout session creation
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Add Stripe Checkout session to confirmOrder: only if org has stripeAccountId and stripeOnboardingComplete. Create session: line_items with order price, payment_intent_data.application_fee_amount=platformFee (order price only, NOT tip), transfer_data.destination=org.stripeAccountId, metadata { platform: fresh-catch, orderId, orderNumber, orgId }, success/cancel URLs. Store stripeCheckoutSessionId. Skip gracefully if Stripe not connected.

## Test Steps
1. Checkout session created with correct amounts
2. application_fee_amount = platformFee
3. transfer_data.destination correct
4. Metadata includes all required fields
5. stripeCheckoutSessionId stored
6. Without Stripe: no session, order still confirms
7. Stripe API error handled gracefully

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

## Commit Message Format
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

title: Implement Voice tool registry + buildCommandPrompt
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Create the voice tool registry that defines all voice actions and generates LLM system prompts. This is the architectural seam: Phase 1 generates prompts, Phase 3 becomes Agents SDK tool definitions.

Create src/api/voice-tools.ts:
- Export voiceTools registry object with these tools:
  update_catch: { description: "Update today's fresh catch with what's available", schema: { headline, items[], summary }, reviewType: "catch" }
  create_market: { description: "Add a new recurring market", schema: { name, schedule, locationDetails?, customerInfo?, catchPreview? }, reviewType: "market-create" }
  create_popup: { description: "Create a temporary popup market event", schema: { name, schedule, expiresAt, locationDetails?, customerInfo?, catchPreview? }, reviewType: "popup-create" }
  update_market: { description: "Update details on an existing market (name match)", schema: { marketId, ...partial fields }, reviewType: "market-update" }
  update_market_catch: { description: "Update what fish are available at a specific market", schema: { marketId, catchPreview }, reviewType: "market-catch" }

- Export function buildCommandPrompt(tools, businessContext):
  - Generates system prompt from registry descriptions + schemas
  - Injects current date (new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]) for relative date resolution
  - Injects business context: list of existing markets (name, id, type, schedule, active)
  - Instructs LLM: return { intent, confidence, data, interpretation }
  - interpretation = human-readable summary ("Update catch preview for Folly Beach Market")
  - If sounds temporary/one-time → type is popup, resolve relative dates to absolute
  - Role-based filtering: accept role param, filter tools by role (admin sees all)

- Export type VoiceCommandResult = { intent: string, confidence: number, data: Record<string, unknown>, interpretation: string }

## Test Steps
1. buildCommandPrompt generates valid prompt containing all tool descriptions
2. Business context (markets list) appears in generated prompt
3. Current date injected correctly
4. Schema shapes described clearly in prompt for LLM
5. Role filtering: non-admin role gets filtered tool list
6. VoiceCommandResult type exported and usable
7. Registry is extensible (adding a new tool is just adding an entry)

## Instructions

1. **Review bearings notes** - Check the bearings step's comments for context
2. **Make focused changes** - Implement one thing at a time
3. **Follow existing patterns** - Match the codebase style
4. **Keep changes minimal** - Don't refactor unrelated code

title: Verify /api/voice/command endpoint
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create the intent-aware voice command endpoint that uses the tool registry and shared voice pipeline.

Create src/api/voice-command.ts — handleVoiceCommand(request, ctx):
- Fetch business context from DB: org's markets (db.market.findMany where organizationId, select id/name/type/schedule/active), current catch status
- Build system prompt via buildCommandPrompt(voiceTools, businessContext)
- Use processVoiceInput() from voice-pipeline.ts with the generated prompt
- Validate response: intent must be a known tool key from voiceTools
- If intent is update_market or update_market_catch: extract marketId from response data, verify it exists in org's markets
- Return: { intent, confidence, data, interpretation, rawTranscript, reviewType }
- reviewType comes from the matched tool in the registry

Register in worker.tsx:
- Add handler after session middleware (alongside existing catch-record handler):
  if (request.method === "POST" && url.pathname === "/api/voice/command") return handleVoiceCommand(request, ctx)

## Test Steps
1. POST audio "got mahi and grouper today" → intent: update_catch, reviewType: catch
2. POST audio "doing popup at Shem Creek Thursday 4-7" → intent: create_popup, expiresAt populated
3. POST audio "heading to Folly Beach, got shrimp" → intent: update_market_catch, marketId matched
4. POST audio "add me to Saturday market downtown" → intent: create_market
5. POST text fallback for all above intents
6. Unknown/ambiguous → low confidence, interpretation explains
7. Auth: 401/403 for unauthorized

title: Verify Wire CommandBar to voice/command endpoint
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Connect the CommandBar FAB to the /api/voice/command endpoint so recording/typing produces a VoiceCommandResult that gets passed to the review layer.

In CommandBar.tsx:
- On audio ready (from useVoiceRecorder onAudioReady callback): POST blob as ArrayBuffer to /api/voice/command with Content-Type of the audio MIME type
- On text submit: POST JSON { text } to /api/voice/command with Content-Type application/json
- Parse response: extract { intent, confidence, data, interpretation, rawTranscript, reviewType }
- Call onResult(result) prop with the parsed response
- Error handling: show error state in half-sheet, retain blob for retry
- Loading state: show processing indicator while waiting for response

In AdminLayout (or parent):
- Receive onResult from CommandBar
- Store result in state
- When result present, render CommandReview component (built in next task) as overlay
- On review save/cancel, clear result state

## Test Steps
1. Record audio in command bar → POST sent to /api/voice/command
2. Response parsed and onResult called with VoiceCommandResult
3. Text mode: type and submit → same endpoint called with JSON
4. Error from endpoint → error state shown in half-sheet
5. Retry after error → blob reused, no re-recording needed
6. Processing indicator visible during API call
7. Result passed to parent triggers review overlay
8. Half-sheet closes when review opens

title: Get bearings for Diff review UI + prominent date picker
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Build the visual diff display for market update reviews and ensure date handling is robust across all popup forms.

In CommandReview.tsx update_market form:
- Diff layout: two-column or stacked showing field name, old value (struck through / dimmed), new value (highlighted)
- Use var(--color-status-success) background tint for changed fields
- Unchanged fields: collapsed below a "Show all fields" toggle, dimmed text
- If user clicks an unchanged field, it becomes editable (promoted to changed)

Date picker for popup dates:
- Use native <input type="date"> for max compatibility (iOS Safari, Chrome, Firefox)
- Prominently placed — not collapsed or hidden. For create_popup: full-width below schedule field
- Pre-populated with LLM's best guess date
- Label: "Popup expires end of day on:"
- Validation: date must be today or future
- For update_market on popups: date picker always visible even if date didn't change

Time display:
- If LLM extracts a time ("4-7pm"), show it in schedule field
- expiresAt always set to 23:59:59 of the selected date (end of day)

## Test Steps
1. Diff view: changed field shows old→new with visual distinction
2. Unchanged fields dimmed and collapsed
3. Click unchanged field → becomes editable
4. Date picker renders as full-width native input
5. Date pre-populated from LLM response
6. Past dates rejected with validation message
7. expiresAt set to end of selected day (23:59:59)
8. Date picker visible on popup update even if date unchanged
9. Mobile: date picker triggers native date selector
10. Time in schedule field preserved separately from date

## STEP 1: Health Check (MANDATORY)

Before exploring for this task, verify the app is healthy:

1. **Start dev server** (if not running) and verify it starts successfully
2. **Run test suite** - Execute existing tests (check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands)
3. **Smoke test via dev-browser** - Navigate to the app and verify a core flow works
   - Use the /dev-browser skill
   - Pick a completed feature from recent work
   - Verify it still functions correctly

### If Health Check FAILS

If ANY of these fail, you CANNOT proceed with this task. Report back to orchestrator:

1. **Identify the cause** - Check git log for recent changes:
   ```bash
   git log --oneline -5
   ```
   Commits should reference bead IDs (e.g., "Refs: beads-xxx")

2. **Document findings** - Add a comment to THIS bead with your investigation

3. **Report to orchestrator** - Do NOT create bug beads or modify epic status yourself.
   The orchestrator will handle creating bug beads and blocking.

## STEP 2: Task-Specific Exploration

Once health check passes:

1. **Search for relevant code** - Use Grep/Glob to find related files
2. **Read key files** - Understand existing patterns and conventions
3. **Identify change locations** - Where will modifications be needed?
4. **Note dependencies** - What other code might be affected?
5. **Document findings** - Add a comment summarizing what you learned

title: Commit Customer FreshSheet Display
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
## Overview
Build the customer-facing FreshSheet component that displays Evan's formatted catch data on the home page. Integrate with the existing home page, replacing the hero section when fresh catch data exists. Include staleness auto-expiry (>7 days falls back to FreshHero).

## Part 1: FreshSheet Component (src/app/pages/home/components/FreshSheet.tsx)

### Design
- Ocean gradient card matching existing FreshHero style language:
  - `background: var(--color-gradient-primary)` (ocean blue → mint)
  - `border-radius: var(--radius-xl)`, `box-shadow: var(--shadow-lg)`
  - White text (--color-text-inverse)
  - Same width/margin as FreshHero: `width: calc(100% - var(--space-md) * 2)`, `max-width: var(--width-md)`, centered

### Content Structure
- "This Week's Catch" label at top (uppercase, small, letter-spaced — same as FreshHero V1 pattern)
- Headline from Claude output (large, bold, --font-size-4xl)
- Item list: each item shows fish name + Evan's descriptive note
  - e.g., "Redfish — beautiful fillets, just pulled in"
  - Use glassmorphic item cards or simple styled list items
  - Each item on its own line, readable
- "Updated X hours ago" relative timestamp at bottom
  - Use simple relative time calculation (no library needed)
  - "just now", "1 hour ago", "2 days ago", etc.
- "Order Now" CTA button at bottom
  - Links to /orders/new
  - Use coral gradient or action-secondary styling for prominence
  - Should stand out as the primary action

### Props Interface
```ts
type CatchData = {
  headline: string;
  items: { name: string; note: string }[];
  summary: string;
  updatedAt: string; // ISO date string
};
```

### Component is NOT "use client"
- This is a presentational component with no interactivity (no useState, no onClick handlers that need client)
- The "Order Now" button is just an `<a>` tag
- Relative timestamp can be computed at render time (server component)
- Keep it as a server component unless client interactivity is needed

## Part 2: Barrel Export
- Add to `src/app/pages/home/components/index.ts`:
  `export { FreshSheet } from './FreshSheet';`

## Part 3: Home Page Integration

### CustomerHome.tsx (server component)
- After fetching markets, also fetch latest live CatchUpdate for the org:
  ```ts
  const catchUpdate = await db.catchUpdate.findFirst({
    where: { organizationId: orgId, status: "live" },
    orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" }
  });
  ```
- Staleness check: only pass if ≤7 days old
  ```ts
  const STALE_DAYS = 7;
  const isStale = catchUpdate && (Date.now() - new Date(catchUpdate.createdAt).getTime()) > STALE_DAYS * 86400000;
  const catchData = catchUpdate && !isStale ? JSON.parse(catchUpdate.formattedContent) : null;
  ```
- Pass `catchData` (with updatedAt from catchUpdate.createdAt) to CustomerHomeUI
- Also pass existing props (markets, freshCatch, quickActions, ctx)

### CustomerHomeUI.tsx (client component)
- Add `catchData` to props type (CatchData | null)
- Conditional render in the hero section:
  - If catchData exists: `<FreshSheet catch={catchData} />`
  - If catchData is null: `<FreshHero actions={quickActions} />` (existing fallback)
- Import FreshSheet from ./components

## Test Steps
1. With live CatchUpdate (≤7d old) in DB: home page shows FreshSheet with formatted catch
2. FreshSheet shows "This Week's Catch" label, headline, items with notes
3. Each item displays fish name and Evan's note (e.g., "Redfish — beautiful fillets")
4. "Updated X hours ago" timestamp is accurate
5. "Order Now" button visible and links to /orders/new
6. With live CatchUpdate >7 days old: home page shows FreshHero fallback (quick actions)
7. Without any CatchUpdate: home page shows FreshHero with quick actions
8. Ocean gradient card matches existing hero aesthetic
9. Mobile: component looks good at 375px width
10. Text is readable (white on gradient, proper contrast)
11. Verify no client-side JavaScript needed (pure server component)

## Context Window Management
If approaching 30-40% context usage with remaining work, create continuation beads for remaining items and exit cleanly.

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

## Commit Message Format
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>
```

Types: feat, fix, refactor, docs, test, chore

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment with the commit details:
```
STATUS: DONE
COMMIT: <commit hash>
MESSAGE: <commit message summary>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to next steps (if any).
```

When complete, close this bead with `bd close <this-bead-id>`.

title: Get bearings for Admin Catch Recording UI
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
## Overview
Build the admin catch recording page (/admin/catch) with a massive dock-friendly mic button, integrate with the audio processing API (built in a prior task), add the dashboard nav card, and register the route. This is the full admin-side UI for voice-to-catch.

## Part 1: Admin Catch Page (Server Component)

### CatchPage.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchPage.tsx)
- Server component following existing pattern (see MarketConfigPage.tsx as reference)
- Auth check: hasAdminAccess(ctx), return appropriate error components if not
- Fetch latest live CatchUpdate for org: `db.catchUpdate.findFirst({ where: { organizationId: ctx.currentOrganization.id, status: "live" }, orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" } })`
- Pass currentCatch (or null) + ctx to CatchUI client component

## Part 2: Admin Catch UI (Client Component)

### CatchUI.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchUI.tsx)
- "use client" component
- State machine with states: idle | recording | processing | done | error | permission-denied

### Mic Button (centerpiece)
- 120px+ diameter circle, `border-radius: var(--radius-full)`
- Idle: ocean blue (`var(--color-action-primary)`), mic icon (SVG or emoji)
- Text below: "Tap to record what's fresh"
- Must be easy to tap with a big thumb on a dock

### Recording State
- Pulsing red ring animation (CSS keyframes)
- 2-minute countdown timer display (mm:ss format)
- Audio level indicator: use AudioContext + AnalyserNode
  - Connect MediaStream source to AnalyserNode
  - Read frequency data in requestAnimationFrame loop
  - Map amplitude to visual feedback (ring glow intensity or thickness pulsing with voice)
  - Evan needs to SEE the mic is picking up sound over dock wind/noise
- Auto-stop AND auto-submit at 0:00 (same behavior as manual stop)
- Text: "Recording... Tap to stop"

### MediaRecorder Integration
- MIME type detection: `MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported()`
  - Prefer `audio/webm;codecs=opus` (Chrome, Android)
  - Fallback: `audio/mp4` (Safari, iOS)
  - Fallback: `audio/webm`
- getUserMedia: `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: { echoCancellation: true, noiseSuppression: true } })`
- On stop: convert blob to ArrayBuffer, POST to /api/catch/record
- Release mic tracks on stop: `stream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())`

### Error State (CRITICAL for dock use)
- RETAIN audio blob in memory on upload failure
- "Retry" button: re-uploads the SAME recording (no re-recording needed)
- "Re-record" button: separate, discards blob, returns to idle
- Error message displayed clearly
- Critical for spotty dock signal — don't lose a 2-minute recording on failure

### Permission Denied State
- Handle navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia rejection
- Show explanation: "Microphone access is needed to record your catch update"
- Instructions to re-enable in browser settings
- Retry button to request permission again

### Processing State
- Spinner animation
- Text: "Transcribing & formatting..."
- Non-interactive (no cancel, let it complete)

### Done State
- Green checkmark or success indicator
- Show formatted catch result (headline, item list, summary)
- "Re-record" button to start over
- "Clear catch" button to archive current live catch without replacing

### Current Live Catch Display
- Below the mic button area
- "Currently showing to customers:" header
- Render the current live catch data if exists
- Show "No catch currently displayed" if none

### Clear Catch
- Button that archives current live catch without recording new one
- Calls clearCatch() server function
- Use case: off-season, or Evan wants to stop showing catch

## Part 3: Server Functions (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch-functions.ts)
- "use server" module
- `clearCatch()`: archives current live catch for org, calls revalidatePath("/")
- Follow existing server function patterns: requestInfo, hasAdminAccess check, return { success, error? }

## Part 4: Styles (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch.css)
- BEM-like naming: `.catch-mic`, `.catch-mic__ring`, `.catch-mic__ring--recording`, `.catch-mic__timer`, `.catch-result`, etc.
- Use semantic design tokens ONLY (var(--color-*), var(--space-*), var(--radius-*))
- @keyframes for pulse animation (recording state)
- @keyframes for amplitude visualization
- Mobile-first: big touch targets, readable on small screens
- @media (max-width: 640px) adjustments if needed

## Part 5: Dashboard Nav Card + Route Registration

### AdminDashboardUI.tsx
- Add 4th nav card to the existing grid:
  - Icon: 🎙️ (microphone emoji)
  - Title: "Fresh Catch"
  - Description: "Record what's available this week"
  - Link: /admin/catch

### routes.ts (src/app/pages/admin/routes.ts)
- Import CatchPage from ./catch/CatchPage
- Add: route("/catch", CatchPage)

## Test Steps
1. Navigate to /admin/catch as admin user — page loads with mic button
2. See big mic button in idle state (120px+, ocean blue)
3. Tap mic — browser requests microphone permission
4. Grant permission — see recording state: red pulsing ring, countdown from 2:00
5. See audio level indicator responding to voice input (visual amplitude)
6. Tap again to stop — see processing spinner ("Transcribing & formatting...")
7. After processing: see formatted catch result with headline, items, summary
8. See "Re-record" button in done state
9. Wait 2 minutes while recording — auto-stops and auto-submits
10. Simulate upload failure: see error with both "Retry" and "Re-record" buttons
11. Tap "Retry" — re-uploads same audio without re-recording
12. Test "Clear catch" — archives current catch
13. Deny mic permission — see helpful permission-denied message with instructions
14. Navigate to /admin — see 4th "Fresh Catch" card with mic icon
15. Click "Fresh Catch" card — navigates to /admin/catch
16. Test on mobile viewport: mic button easy to tap with thumb
17. Verify all styles use semantic design tokens (no hardcoded hex)

If approaching 30-40% context usage with remaining work, create continuation beads for remaining items and exit cleanly.

title: Implement Customer home popup integration
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Integrate popup cards into customer home page and add catch preview support to regular market cards.

CustomerHome.tsx (server component):
- Fetch active popups separately:
  where: { organizationId, type: "popup", active: true, cancelledAt: null }
  orderBy: { expiresAt: "asc" } (soonest first)
- Filter in code: expiresAt is null OR expiresAt >= start of today
- Serialize for client: convert dates to ISO strings
- Pass popups array to CustomerHomeUI

CustomerHomeUI.tsx (client component):
- New section above regular markets: "Coming Up" or "Popup Events"
- Only rendered if popups array is non-empty
- Render PopupCard for each active popup
- Multiple popups on same day: stack as separate cards

Regular MarketCard enhancement:
- If market has catchPreview (JSON string), parse and display
- Subtle treatment: below schedule, small text: "Usually available: Mahi, Grouper, Shrimp"
- Just item names, no notes (keep compact for regular cards)
- Only show if catchPreview has items

Small link below popup section: "See past popups →" → /markets/past (even if archive page not built yet, link is ready)

## Test Steps
1. Active popup appears in "Coming Up" section above markets
2. Expired popup not shown (expiresAt < today filtered out)
3. Cancelled popup not shown (cancelledAt filter)
4. Multiple popups render as stacked cards
5. Popups sorted soonest-first
6. Empty popup section hidden entirely
7. Regular market with catchPreview shows "Usually available" line
8. Regular market without catchPreview shows no extra line
9. "See past popups" link present when popups section shows
10. Page renders correctly with zero popups (no section)
11. Page renders with mix of popups and regular markets

title: Verify Refactor CatchUI to use shared voice infra
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Refactor existing CatchUI.tsx and catch-record.ts to use the new shared infrastructure (useVoiceRecorder hook, VoiceMicButton component, voice-pipeline.ts). This proves the shared abstractions work correctly.

CatchUI.tsx changes:
- Replace inline MediaRecorder/AudioContext/AnalyserNode code with useVoiceRecorder hook
- Replace inline mic button JSX+CSS with VoiceMicButton component
- Keep: review form, publish logic, catch-specific state (review/publishing/done), text mode toggle
- CatchUI still owns its state machine but delegates recording to the hook

catch-record.ts changes:
- Replace inline auth/Whisper/LLM/parse code with processVoiceInput() from voice-pipeline.ts
- Becomes thin wrapper: provides catch-specific system prompt + validateCatchContent
- Keep: CatchContent interface, catch-specific system prompt text

Reference files:
- src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchUI.tsx (refactor target)
- src/api/catch-record.ts (refactor target)
- src/hooks/useVoiceRecorder.ts (new dependency)
- src/components/VoiceMicButton.tsx (new dependency)
- src/api/voice-pipeline.ts (new dependency)

## Test Steps
1. Navigate to /admin/catch — mic button renders (now VoiceMicButton)
2. Record audio → processing → review screen with headline, items, summary
3. Edit items in review → changes reflected
4. Publish → catch saved, success state
5. Text mode toggle → type description → same review flow
6. Error state → retry works with retained blob
7. iOS Safari: mp4 recording works
8. Chrome: webm recording works
9. Amplitude visualization responsive during recording
10. 120s auto-stop still triggers
11. No visual regressions compared to before refactor

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)

title: Implement Admin Catch Recording UI
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
## Overview
Build the admin catch recording page (/admin/catch) with a massive dock-friendly mic button, integrate with the audio processing API (built in a prior task), add the dashboard nav card, and register the route. This is the full admin-side UI for voice-to-catch.

## Part 1: Admin Catch Page (Server Component)

### CatchPage.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchPage.tsx)
- Server component following existing pattern (see MarketConfigPage.tsx as reference)
- Auth check: hasAdminAccess(ctx), return appropriate error components if not
- Fetch latest live CatchUpdate for org: `db.catchUpdate.findFirst({ where: { organizationId: ctx.currentOrganization.id, status: "live" }, orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" } })`
- Pass currentCatch (or null) + ctx to CatchUI client component

## Part 2: Admin Catch UI (Client Component)

### CatchUI.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchUI.tsx)
- "use client" component
- State machine with states: idle | recording | processing | done | error | permission-denied

### Mic Button (centerpiece)
- 120px+ diameter circle, `border-radius: var(--radius-full)`
- Idle: ocean blue (`var(--color-action-primary)`), mic icon (SVG or emoji)
- Text below: "Tap to record what's fresh"
- Must be easy to tap with a big thumb on a dock

### Recording State
- Pulsing red ring animation (CSS keyframes)
- 2-minute countdown timer display (mm:ss format)
- Audio level indicator: use AudioContext + AnalyserNode
  - Connect MediaStream source to AnalyserNode
  - Read frequency data in requestAnimationFrame loop
  - Map amplitude to visual feedback (ring glow intensity or thickness pulsing with voice)
  - Evan needs to SEE the mic is picking up sound over dock wind/noise
- Auto-stop AND auto-submit at 0:00 (same behavior as manual stop)
- Text: "Recording... Tap to stop"

### MediaRecorder Integration
- MIME type detection: `MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported()`
  - Prefer `audio/webm;codecs=opus` (Chrome, Android)
  - Fallback: `audio/mp4` (Safari, iOS)
  - Fallback: `audio/webm`
- getUserMedia: `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: { echoCancellation: true, noiseSuppression: true } })`
- On stop: convert blob to ArrayBuffer, POST to /api/catch/record
- Release mic tracks on stop: `stream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())`

### Error State (CRITICAL for dock use)
- RETAIN audio blob in memory on upload failure
- "Retry" button: re-uploads the SAME recording (no re-recording needed)
- "Re-record" button: separate, discards blob, returns to idle
- Error message displayed clearly
- Critical for spotty dock signal — don't lose a 2-minute recording on failure

### Permission Denied State
- Handle navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia rejection
- Show explanation: "Microphone access is needed to record your catch update"
- Instructions to re-enable in browser settings
- Retry button to request permission again

### Processing State
- Spinner animation
- Text: "Transcribing & formatting..."
- Non-interactive (no cancel, let it complete)

### Done State
- Green checkmark or success indicator
- Show formatted catch result (headline, item list, summary)
- "Re-record" button to start over
- "Clear catch" button to archive current live catch without replacing

### Current Live Catch Display
- Below the mic button area
- "Currently showing to customers:" header
- Render the current live catch data if exists
- Show "No catch currently displayed" if none

### Clear Catch
- Button that archives current live catch without recording new one
- Calls clearCatch() server function
- Use case: off-season, or Evan wants to stop showing catch

## Part 3: Server Functions (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch-functions.ts)
- "use server" module
- `clearCatch()`: archives current live catch for org, calls revalidatePath("/")
- Follow existing server function patterns: requestInfo, hasAdminAccess check, return { success, error? }

## Part 4: Styles (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch.css)
- BEM-like naming: `.catch-mic`, `.catch-mic__ring`, `.catch-mic__ring--recording`, `.catch-mic__timer`, `.catch-result`, etc.
- Use semantic design tokens ONLY (var(--color-*), var(--space-*), var(--radius-*))
- @keyframes for pulse animation (recording state)
- @keyframes for amplitude visualization
- Mobile-first: big touch targets, readable on small screens
- @media (max-width: 640px) adjustments if needed

## Part 5: Dashboard Nav Card + Route Registration

### AdminDashboardUI.tsx
- Add 4th nav card to the existing grid:
  - Icon: 🎙️ (microphone emoji)
  - Title: "Fresh Catch"
  - Description: "Record what's available this week"
  - Link: /admin/catch

### routes.ts (src/app/pages/admin/routes.ts)
- Import CatchPage from ./catch/CatchPage
- Add: route("/catch", CatchPage)

## Test Steps
1. Navigate to /admin/catch as admin user — page loads with mic button
2. See big mic button in idle state (120px+, ocean blue)
3. Tap mic — browser requests microphone permission
4. Grant permission — see recording state: red pulsing ring, countdown from 2:00
5. See audio level indicator responding to voice input (visual amplitude)
6. Tap again to stop — see processing spinner ("Transcribing & formatting...")
7. After processing: see formatted catch result with headline, items, summary
8. See "Re-record" button in done state
9. Wait 2 minutes while recording — auto-stops and auto-submits
10. Simulate upload failure: see error with both "Retry" and "Re-record" buttons
11. Tap "Retry" — re-uploads same audio without re-recording
12. Test "Clear catch" — archives current catch
13. Deny mic permission — see helpful permission-denied message with instructions
14. Navigate to /admin — see 4th "Fresh Catch" card with mic icon
15. Click "Fresh Catch" card — navigates to /admin/catch
16. Test on mobile viewport: mic button easy to tap with thumb
17. Verify all styles use semantic design tokens (no hardcoded hex)

## Context Window Management
If approaching 30-40% context usage with remaining work, create continuation beads for remaining items and exit cleanly.

## Instructions

2. **Make focused changes** - Implement one thing at a time
3. **Follow existing patterns** - Match the codebase style
4. **Keep changes minimal** - Don't refactor unrelated code
5. **Add necessary tests** - If the codebase has tests, add relevant ones

title: Webhook: payment success handlers
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Webhook: payment success handlers

## Task
**Category:** infrastructure

Implement webhook handlers: checkout.session.completed -> extract orderId from metadata, create Payment record (amount, method=stripe, type based on deposit/full), increment order.amountPaid, set paidAt if fully paid, store stripePaymentIntentId. payment_intent.succeeded as backup (skip if Payment already exists). title: Get bearings for Market context injection for voice updates
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Enhance the /api/voice/command endpoint to provide richer context when updating existing markets, enabling partial-change detection.

In src/api/voice-command.ts:
- When the LLM returns intent update_market or update_market_catch with a marketId:
  - Fetch full market from DB (all fields including catchPreview)
  - Option A (simpler): The initial prompt already includes all markets with their current state, so the LLM can compute the diff in one pass. Ensure buildCommandPrompt includes enough detail per market (name, schedule, type, active, subtitle, catchPreview summary)
  - Option B (two-pass, more accurate): After first pass identifies intent + target market, re-prompt with full market state asking for only changed fields. This is more token-expensive but more reliable.
  - Start with Option A. If field extraction quality is poor, upgrade to Option B.

- Market name matching: LLM returns marketId from the injected market list. If the market list includes IDs, the LLM can return the exact ID. If fuzzy matching needed, compare LLM-returned name against market names using simple substring/lowercase match.

- For update_market_catch: merge new catch preview with any existing catch preview if needed, or full replace

- Accept optional query param ?marketId=xxx for explicit targeting (e.g., from admin UI "update this market" button)

## Test Steps
1. "Folly Beach moved to Sundays" → marketId correctly matched, only schedule in changed data
2. "Market's paused until April" → active=false and subtitle in changed data
3. "Got shrimp for Folly Beach" → update_market_catch with correct marketId
4. Explicit ?marketId param → uses that market regardless of voice content
5. Market not found → graceful error
6. Ambiguous name (multiple partial matches) → returns lower confidence
7. Full market state available in prompt context

title: Implement CommandReview + catch/market/popup forms
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal

## Task Context
Create the polymorphic review component that renders different forms based on the voice command intent.

Create src/components/CommandReview.tsx ("use client"):
- Props: { intent, data, interpretation, rawTranscript, onSave, onCancel, onRetry }
- Header: shows interpretation text ("Updating catch preview for Folly Beach Market")
- Renders as full-screen overlay (half-sheet transitions to full for review space)

Intent-specific forms (switch on intent/reviewType):

- create_market (reviewType: "market-create"): name input, schedule input, locationDetails textarea, customerInfo textarea, catchPreview items editor, active toggle
- create_popup (reviewType: "popup-create"): same as market-create PLUS: expiresAt date picker (PROMINENT — large, not hidden), type shown as "Popup" badge, notes field if populated

title: Get bearings for Review: update diff, confidence, save wiring
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Add market update diff view, low-confidence warning, and wire all review forms to their server functions.

In CommandReview.tsx, add:

update_market (reviewType: "market-update") form:
- Diff view: changed fields highlighted (old→new values shown), unchanged fields dimmed below
- User can toggle unchanged fields to edit them too
- Date picker always visible for popup expiresAt changes

update_market_catch (reviewType: "market-catch") form:
- Market name shown read-only (matched by voice)
- catchPreview items editable (add/remove, name+note per item)

Confidence warning (all intents):
- If confidence < 0.7: show yellow warning banner: "I'm not sure about this. Did you mean: [interpretation]?"
- "That's not right" button → options: re-record, or manual intent selection dropdown
- Manual intent selection: dropdown of all tool descriptions, selecting one re-renders the appropriate form with existing data

Save wiring (all intents):
- Save button calls the appropriate server function based on intent:
  update_catch → publishCatch(content, rawTranscript) from catch-functions.ts
  create_market → createMarket(data) from market-functions.ts
  create_popup → createMarket({...data, type: "popup"}) from market-functions.ts
  update_market → updateMarket(marketId, changedFields) from market-functions.ts
  update_market_catch → updateMarketCatchPreview(marketId, catchPreview, rawTranscript) from market-functions.ts
- On save success: close overlay, show brief success toast, trigger page refresh/revalidation
- On save error: show error in review, don't close

## Test Steps
1. update_market intent → diff view shows changed vs unchanged fields
2. Changed fields highlighted, old→new values visible
3. update_market_catch intent → market name read-only, items editable
4. Low confidence (<0.7) → warning banner shown
5. "That's not right" → re-record option works
6. Manual intent selection dropdown appears and re-renders form
7. Save update_catch → publishCatch called, success toast
8. Save create_popup → createMarket called with type="popup"
9. Save update_market → updateMarket called with only changed fields
10. Save error → error shown, overlay stays open
11. Success → overlay closes, page refreshes

title: Commit Extend market server functions for popups
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Update src/app/pages/admin/market-functions.ts to handle popup-specific fields, lifecycle actions, and catch preview for both market types.

Changes to existing functions:
- Update FIELD_LIMITS: add catchPreview (2000), notes (1000), rawTranscript (5000)
- Update createMarket() params: accept type, expiresAt, catchPreview, notes, rawTranscript
- Update updateMarket() params: accept same new fields with conditional spreads pattern
- Update validateMarketFields() for new field limits

New functions:
- cancelPopup(id): Sets cancelledAt to now(), active to false. Keeps record for admin history but hidden from customer archive.
- endPopup(id): Sets active to false. cancelledAt stays null (it happened, just ended early).
- updateMarketCatchPreview(id, catchPreview, rawTranscript): Updates just catch preview fields on existing market. Used by update_market_catch intent.

New helpers:
- isPopupExpired(market): market.type === "popup" && market.expiresAt && market.expiresAt < now
- isPopupCancelled(market): market.cancelledAt !== null

All functions follow existing pattern: "use server", requestInfo for context, hasAdminAccess guard, organization scoping.

## Test Steps
1. createMarket with type="popup", expiresAt, catchPreview → saved correctly
2. createMarket with no new fields → type defaults to "regular", others null
3. updateMarket with new fields → conditional spreads work
4. cancelPopup → cancelledAt set, active=false
5. endPopup → active=false, cancelledAt stays null
6. updateMarketCatchPreview → only catchPreview + rawTranscript updated
7. Field validation: catchPreview over 2000 chars rejected
8. All functions require admin access (throw on non-admin)

## Instructions

1. **Review changes** - Run `git diff` to see all modifications
2. **Stage relevant files** - Only include files related to this task
3. **Write commit message** - Follow conventional commits format
4. **Create commit** - Use the commit skill or git directly

<type>(<scope>): <description>

<body with details>

Refs: <bead-id>

## Goal

## Task Context
Extract shared voice recording infrastructure into reusable modules:

Create src/hooks/useVoiceRecorder.ts:
- Encapsulates: MediaRecorder lifecycle, AudioContext + AnalyserNode, amplitude state, timer (configurable duration, default 120s), blob management + retention on error, MIME type detection (webm/mp4), cleanup
- Returns: { state, amplitude, timeLeft, startRecording, stopRecording, retryUpload, blob }
- State type: "idle" | "recording" | "processing" | "done" | "error" | "permission-denied"
- Accepts: onAudioReady(blob) callback for upload handling

Create src/components/VoiceMicButton.tsx + voice-mic.css:
- 140px circular mic button with amplitude-reactive glow
- Timer display, state-based styling (idle=blue, recording=red, processing=spinner)
- Pulsing ring animation during recording
- Reuses catch.css patterns, uses design tokens
- Props: state, amplitude, timeLeft, onTap (start/stop toggle)

Reference: src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchUI.tsx lines 38-91 for existing MediaRecorder/AudioContext code, src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch.css for mic button styles.

## Test Steps
1. Hook initializes with idle state
2. startRecording requests mic permission and enters recording state
3. Amplitude values update during recording (0-1 range)
4. Timer counts down from configured duration
5. Auto-stop triggers at 0
6. stopRecording produces a Blob
7. Blob retained on error for retry
8. MIME type detection: webm on Chrome, mp4 on Safari
9. Cleanup disposes AudioContext and MediaRecorder
10. VoiceMicButton renders correctly in all states (idle, recording, processing, error)
11. Amplitude glow responsive during recording

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)

Your ID is in the bead data you received.

title: Commit Refactor CatchUI to use shared voice infra
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Refactor existing CatchUI.tsx and catch-record.ts to use the new shared infrastructure (useVoiceRecorder hook, VoiceMicButton component, voice-pipeline.ts). This proves the shared abstractions work correctly.

CatchUI.tsx changes:
- Replace inline MediaRecorder/AudioContext/AnalyserNode code with useVoiceRecorder hook
- Replace inline mic button JSX+CSS with VoiceMicButton component
- Keep: review form, publish logic, catch-specific state (review/publishing/done), text mode toggle
- CatchUI still owns its state machine but delegates recording to the hook

catch-record.ts changes:
- Replace inline auth/Whisper/LLM/parse code with processVoiceInput() from voice-pipeline.ts
- Becomes thin wrapper: provides catch-specific system prompt + validateCatchContent
- Keep: CatchContent interface, catch-specific system prompt text

Reference files:
- src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchUI.tsx (refactor target)
- src/api/catch-record.ts (refactor target)
- src/hooks/useVoiceRecorder.ts (new dependency)
- src/components/VoiceMicButton.tsx (new dependency)

title: Implement PopupCard customer component
description:
# IMPLEMENTATION PHASE

## Goal
Make the code changes to implement the feature/fix.

## Task Context
Create the customer-facing popup card with urgency styling, catch preview, and action CTA.

Create src/app/pages/home/components/PopupCard.tsx:
- Props: { popup: { name, schedule, expiresAt, locationDetails?, customerInfo?, catchPreview? } }
- Visual treatment (distinct from regular MarketCard):
  - Background: coral/warm gradient (var(--color-gradient-secondary))
  - Text: var(--color-text-inverse) (white on gradient)
  - Border radius: var(--radius-xl)
  - Box shadow: var(--shadow-coral) or var(--shadow-lg)

- Badge (top-right or top area):
  - If expiresAt is today: "TODAY!" in bold, possibly animated subtle pulse
  - If expiresAt is tomorrow: "TOMORROW!"
  - Otherwise: formatted date (e.g., "Thu, Apr 2")
  - Badge styling: small pill, contrasting background

- Content:
  - Market name (large, bold)
  - Schedule/time (e.g., "4pm - 7pm")
  - Location (if locationDetails provided)
  - Catch preview items: frosted glass cards (rgba white bg with blur, like FreshSheet items)

title: Get bearings for Market popup schema migration
description:
# BEARINGS PHASE

## Goal
1. Verify the codebase is in a healthy state
2. Understand the relevant code for this task

## Task Context
Add popup and voice fields to Market model in prisma/schema.prisma. New fields (all optional/defaulted for backward compat):
- type String @default("regular") // "regular" | "popup"
- expiresAt DateTime?
- catchPreview String? // JSON: { items: [{name, note}] }
- notes String? // catch-all for unstructured voice content
- rawTranscript String? // verbatim Whisper output (null for form-created)
- cancelledAt DateTime? // set if popup cancelled before happening
- Add index: @@index([organizationId, type, active])
- Create migration: pnpm run migrate:new (name: add-popup-market-fields)
- Apply locally: pnpm run migrate:dev
- Regenerate types: pnpm run generate

## Test Steps
1. pnpm run migrate:dev — no errors
2. pnpm run generate — Prisma client regenerated
3. pnpm run types — no type errors
4. Existing market CRUD still works (all new fields optional/defaulted)
5. Can create a market with type="popup" and expiresAt set

## STEP 1: Health Check (MANDATORY)

Before exploring for this task, verify the app is healthy:

1. **Start dev server** (if not running) and verify it starts successfully
2. **Run test suite** - Execute existing tests (check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands)

   - Verify it still functions correctly

2. **Document findings** - Add a comment to THIS bead with your investigation

3. **Report to orchestrator** - Do NOT create bug beads or modify epic status yourself.
   The orchestrator will handle creating bug beads and blocking.

## STEP 2: Task-Specific Exploration

Once health check passes:

1. **Search for relevant code** - Use Grep/Glob to find related files
2. **Read key files** - Understand existing patterns and conventions

title: Verify Cancel/end popup actions + modal popup fields
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Add cancel/end actions on popup cards and extend MarketFormModal to handle popup-specific fields.

Popup card actions in MarketConfigUI:
- Active popup card gets two new action buttons:
  - "End Now" → calls endPopup(id), moves to inactive section
  - "Cancel" → calls cancelPopup(id), moves to inactive with "Cancelled" badge
- Confirmation: brief inline confirm ("End this popup?" [Yes] [No]) — not a modal-in-a-modal
- Use useTransition for optimistic updates (existing pattern)

MarketFormModal extension (src/app/pages/admin/components/MarketFormModal.tsx):
- Add type toggle: "Regular Market" | "Popup" (radio or segmented control)
- When type=popup, show additional fields:
  - expiresAt: date picker (native <input type="date">), required for popups
  - catchPreview: item list editor (add/remove items, each with name + note inputs)
  - notes: textarea (shown if populated or user clicks "Add notes")
- When type=regular, hide popup-specific fields (expiresAt, etc.)
- catchPreview editor also available for regular markets ("Usually available" fish)
- Form validation: popup requires expiresAt to be set and in future
- On save: include all new fields in createMarket/updateMarket call

## Test Steps
1. "End Now" on active popup → endPopup called, card moves to inactive
2. "Cancel" on popup → cancelPopup called, cancelledAt set, "Cancelled" badge
3. Confirmation inline prompt works (not a nested modal)
4. MarketFormModal: type toggle switches between regular/popup
5. Popup mode shows expiresAt picker + catchPreview editor
6. Regular mode hides expiresAt, shows optional catchPreview
7. catchPreview item editor: add item, remove item, edit name+note
8. Validation: popup without expiresAt shows error
9. Validation: past date rejected
10. Save popup → all new fields included in server function call

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)

comments.text:
STATUS: HEALTHY (pre-existing TS errors in JoinUI.tsx, unrelated to admin.css)

FILES: src/app/pages/admin/admin.css (only file to modify)

HARDCODED HEX VALUES FOUND (with line numbers):
1. L32: rgba(0, 102, 204, 0.1) — border color, already an rgba but uses raw #0066CC equiv
2. L133: #6B7280 — fallback for config-subtitle color (followed by var(--cool-gray))
3. L145: white — fallback for markets-list-container bg
4. L148: #E8EFF5 — fallback border color (followed by rgba override)
5. L165: white — fallback for market-item bg
6. L170: #F3F4F6 — fallback for market-item--inactive bg (followed by var(--light-gray))
7. L176: #E8EFF5 — fallback for market-item border-bottom
8. L195: #1A1A2E — fallback for market-item__name color (followed by var(--deep-navy))
9. L206: #6B7280 — fallback for market-item__subtitle (followed by var(--cool-gray))
10. L212: #6B7280 — fallback for market-item__schedule (followed by var(--cool-gray))
11. L222: white — fallback for config-empty-state bg
12. L227: #CBD5E1 — fallback for config-empty-state dashed border
13. L229: #6B7280 — fallback for config-empty-state color (followed by var(--cool-gray))
14. L241: #1A1A2E — fallback for config-empty-state__title (followed by var(--deep-navy))

OLD FLAT TOKEN REFS (should be semantic):
- L32: rgba(0,102,204,0.1) → var(--color-border-subtle)
- L134: var(--cool-gray) → var(--color-text-secondary)
- L146: var(--surface-primary) → var(--color-surface-primary)
- L149: rgba(100,116,139,0.1) → var(--color-border-subtle)
- L166: var(--surface-primary) → var(--color-surface-primary)
- L171: var(--light-gray) → var(--color-surface-secondary)
- L177: rgba(100,116,139,0.1) → var(--color-border-subtle)
- L196: var(--deep-navy) → var(--color-text-primary)
- L207: var(--cool-gray) → var(--color-text-secondary)
- L213: var(--cool-gray) → var(--color-text-secondary)
- L223: var(--surface-primary) → var(--color-surface-primary)
- L228: rgba(100,116,139,0.2) → var(--color-border-medium)
- L230: var(--cool-gray) → var(--color-text-secondary)
- L242: var(--deep-navy) → var(--color-text-primary)

MAPPING (hex fallback + old flat → semantic):
#6B7280 / var(--cool-gray)         → var(--color-text-secondary)
#1A1A2E / var(--deep-navy)         → var(--color-text-primary)
#F3F4F6 / var(--light-gray)        → var(--color-surface-secondary)
white / var(--surface-primary)      → var(--color-surface-primary)
#E8EFF5                             → var(--color-border-input) [already defined as #E8EFF5]
#CBD5E1                             → var(--color-border-input-disabled) [already defined as #CBD5E1]
rgba(0,102,204,0.1)                 → var(--color-border-subtle)
rgba(100,116,139,0.1)               → var(--color-border-subtle)
rgba(100,116,139,0.2)               → var(--color-border-medium)

APPROACH: Remove hex fallback lines entirely (the pattern is: hex on one line, var() override on next — just keep the var() line). Replace old flat token refs with semantic aliases. The rgba() border values map to existing semantic border tokens. Total: ~28 replacements across one file.

CONCERNS:
- #1A1A2E != #1A2B3D (deep-navy). The hex #1A1A2E is not the actual deep-navy value. Using var(--color-text-primary) which resolves to var(--deep-navy)=#1A2B3D is a slight color shift but correct per the design system.
- The hex+var() fallback pattern (hex first, then var() override) was for browser compat. Removing the hex line is safe since CSS custom properties have near-universal support now.
- Pre-existing TS errors in JoinUI.tsx (autoFocus prop) are unrelated and won't block this work.

comments.author: ptre
comments.text:
STATUS: HEALTHY
FILES: src/app/pages/admin/admin.css (single file)
PATTERNS: File uses hex fallback + var() override pattern on consecutive lines. Also has old flat token refs (--cool-gray, --deep-navy, etc.)
APPROACH: (1) Delete hex fallback lines where var() line exists below, (2) Replace standalone hex values with semantic tokens, (3) Upgrade old flat tokens to semantic names. ~28 edits total.
CONCERNS: #1A1A2E differs slightly from --deep-navy (#1A2B3D) - minor color shift when removing fallback. Pre-existing TS error in JoinUI.tsx unrelated.

  - Background: coral/warm gradient (var(--color-gradient-secondary))
  - Text: var(--color-text-inverse) (white on gradient)
  - Border radius: var(--radius-xl)
  - Box shadow: var(--shadow-coral) or var(--shadow-lg)

- Badge (top-right or top area):
  - If expiresAt is today: "TODAY!" in bold, possibly animated subtle pulse
  - If expiresAt is tomorrow: "TOMORROW!"
  - Otherwise: formatted date (e.g., "Thu, Apr 2")
  - Badge styling: small pill, contrasting background

- Content:
  - Market name (large, bold)
  - Schedule/time (e.g., "4pm - 7pm")
  - Location (if locationDetails provided)
  - Catch preview items: frosted glass cards (rgba white bg with blur, like FreshSheet items)
    Each item: name (bold) + note
  - Customer info (if provided, subtle text)

- CTA: "Order Now" button → /orders/new
  - Styled as prominent button, contrasting with card gradient

- Export from src/app/pages/home/components/index.ts

## Test Steps
1. PopupCard renders with coral gradient, visually distinct from MarketCard
2. TODAY badge when expiresAt is today
3. TOMORROW badge when expiresAt is tomorrow
4. Future date formatted correctly
5. Catch preview items render with frosted glass effect
6. Market name, time, location all displayed
7. "Order Now" links to /orders/new
8. Renders well on mobile (single column, full width)
9. Handles missing optional fields gracefully (no location, no catch preview)
10. White text readable on coral gradient

title: Commit Market popup schema migration
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
Add popup and voice fields to Market model in prisma/schema.prisma. New fields (all optional/defaulted for backward compat):
- type String @default("regular") // "regular" | "popup"
- expiresAt DateTime?
- catchPreview String? // JSON: { items: [{name, note}] }
- notes String? // catch-all for unstructured voice content
- rawTranscript String? // verbatim Whisper output (null for form-created)
- cancelledAt DateTime? // set if popup cancelled before happening
- Add index: @@index([organizationId, type, active])
- Create migration: pnpm run migrate:new (name: add-popup-market-fields)
- Apply locally: pnpm run migrate:dev
- Regenerate types: pnpm run generate

## Test Steps

2. pnpm run generate — Prisma client regenerated

title: Verify Update OrderConfirmed email with price breakdown and Pay Now
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Update src/emails/OrderConfirmed.tsx: add price breakdown (Items, Platform fee if customer/split, Total). Add 'Pay Now' button linking to checkout URL (only if provided). Deposit info if applicable. Keep existing content. 'or pay at pickup' below button. Graceful without Stripe.

## Test Steps
1. Email includes price breakdown
2. Fee shown for customer absorb
3. Fee hidden for vendor absorb
4. Pay Now button with correct URL
5. No button when no checkoutUrl
6. Deposit info shown
7. 'or pay at pickup' present
8. Existing content preserved

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)
2. **Run tests** - Execute test suite (e.g., `bun test`)
3. **Run linting** - If available, run the linter

## UI Verification (if applicable)

If this task involves UI changes, use the /dev-browser skill:

1. Navigate to the affected page
2. Follow any test steps from the task description
3. Save screenshots to `.choo-choo-ralph/screenshots/` for verification
4. Check for:
   - Visual regressions (layout issues, overflow)
   - Console errors
   - White-on-white text or poor contrast
   - Responsiveness issues

## If Verification FAILS

### Small Fixes (typos, minor issues)
If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly
2. Re-run verification
3. Continue if passes

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)
If the issue requires substantial rework, report back to orchestrator with FAIL status.
Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist
- [ ] Types pass
- [ ] Tests pass (existing + new)
- [ ] Linting passes
- [ ] UI verification (if applicable)
- [ ] Code review looks good

## Output to Orchestrator

Add a comment to THIS bead with verification results, then report to orchestrator.

**If all checks pass**:
```
STATUS: PASS
TYPES: pass
TESTS: pass
LINT: pass (or N/A)
UI: pass (or N/A)
SCREENSHOTS: <paths if applicable>

ACTION: Close this step and proceed to commit.
```

**If checks fail (significant issues)**:
```
STATUS: FAIL
TYPES: pass/fail
TESTS: pass/fail
LINT: pass/fail
UI: pass/fail
ISSUE: <what's wrong>
SUGGESTION: <what implement should try differently>

ACTION: You must handle this verification failure:
1. Count `[attempt-N]` comments on your bead: `bd comments <your-id>`
2. If < 3 attempts:
   - Add attempt comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[attempt-N] Implementation attempt N: FAIL - <ISSUE from above>"`
   - Reopen implement step: `bd update <implement-step-id> --status open`
   - Add guidance: `bd comments add <implement-step-id> "[REWORK] <SUGGESTION from above>"`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Wait for implement to complete, then verify again
3. If >= 3 attempts:
   - Add critical comment: `bd comments add <your-id> "[CRITICAL] Implementation failed after 3 attempts. Requires human review."`
   - Block yourself: `bd update <your-id> --status blocked`
   - Close this verify step: `bd close <this-step-id>`
   - Exit
```

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work that's clearly needed (validation, security, integration glue, missing API connections):
```bash
bd comments add <root-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description with file context>"
```

## Capturing Learnings
If you encountered something noteworthy:
- An error you struggled with and how you fixed it
- A pattern you had to follow that wasn't documented
- A library/component usage gotcha future agents should know
- Something that might warrant a skill (e.g., "always use X when doing Y")

```bash
bd comments add <root-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"
```

Only add gaps/learnings for non-obvious issues that would help future agents.

title: /api/voice/command endpoint
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: /api/voice/command endpoint

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Create the intent-aware voice command endpoint that uses the tool registry and shared voice pipeline.

Create src/api/voice-command.ts — handleVoiceCommand(request, ctx):
- Fetch business context from DB: org's markets (db.market.findMany where organizationId, select id/name/type/schedule/active), current catch status
- Build system prompt via buildCommandPrompt(voiceTools, businessContext)
- Use processVoiceInput() from voice-pipeline.ts with the generated prompt
- Validate response: intent must be a known tool key from voiceTools
- If intent is update_market or update_market_catch: extract marketId from response data, verify it exists in org's markets
- Return: { intent, confidence, data, interpretation, rawTranscript, reviewType }
- reviewType comes from the matched tool in the registry

Register in worker.tsx:
- Add handler after session middleware (alongside existing catch-record handler):
  if (request.method === "POST" && url.pathname === "/api/voice/command") return handleVoiceCommand(request, ctx)

## Test Steps
1. POST audio "got mahi and grouper today" → intent: update_catch, reviewType: catch
2. POST audio "doing popup at Shem Creek Thursday 4-7" → intent: create_popup, expiresAt populated
3. POST audio "heading to Folly Beach, got shrimp" → intent: update_market_catch, marketId matched
4. POST audio "add me to Saturday market downtown" → intent: create_market
5. POST text fallback for all above intents
6. Unknown/ambiguous → low confidence, interpretation explains
7. Auth: 401/403 for unauthorized
8. Markets list correctly fetched and injected as context
9. Invalid intent from LLM → error handled gracefully

title: Review: update diff, confidence, save wiring
description:
# ORCHESTRATOR: Review: update diff, confidence, save wiring

You coordinate child steps for this task.

## Task
**Category:** functional

Add market update diff view, low-confidence warning, and wire all review forms to their server functions.

In CommandReview.tsx, add:

update_market (reviewType: "market-update") form:
- Diff view: changed fields highlighted (old→new values shown), unchanged fields dimmed below
- User can toggle unchanged fields to edit them too
- Date picker always visible for popup expiresAt changes

update_market_catch (reviewType: "market-catch") form:
- Market name shown read-only (matched by voice)
- catchPreview items editable (add/remove, name+note per item)

Confidence warning (all intents):
- If confidence < 0.7: show yellow warning banner: "I'm not sure about this. Did you mean: [interpretation]?"
- "That's not right" button → options: re-record, or manual intent selection dropdown
- Manual intent selection: dropdown of all tool descriptions, selecting one re-renders the appropriate form with existing data

Save wiring (all intents):
- Save button calls the appropriate server function based on intent:
  update_catch → publishCatch(content, rawTranscript) from catch-functions.ts
  create_market → createMarket(data) from market-functions.ts
  create_popup → createMarket({...data, type: "popup"}) from market-functions.ts
  update_market → updateMarket(marketId, changedFields) from market-functions.ts
  update_market_catch → updateMarketCatchPreview(marketId, catchPreview, rawTranscript) from market-functions.ts
- On save success: close overlay, show brief success toast, trigger page refresh/revalidation
- On save error: show error in review, don't close

## Test Steps
1. update_market intent → diff view shows changed vs unchanged fields
2. Changed fields highlighted, old→new values visible
3. update_market_catch intent → market name read-only, items editable
4. Low confidence (<0.7) → warning banner shown
5. "That's not right" → re-record option works
6. Manual intent selection dropdown appears and re-renders form
7. Save update_catch → publishCatch called, success toast
8. Save create_popup → createMarket called with type="popup"
9. Save update_market → updateMarket called with only changed fields
10. Save error → error shown, overlay stays open
11. Success → overlay closes, page refreshes

## Execution Loop

title: Verify Money utility functions
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Add money utilities (src/utils/stripe.ts or src/utils/money.ts): formatCents(cents) returns '$45.00'. parseDollars(input) parses '$45.50' or '45' to 4550/4500. calculatePlatformFee(priceCents, feeBps, feeModel) returns { customerTotal, vendorReceives, platformFee }. feeModel customer: fee on top. vendor: fee from share. split: 50/50. All integer cents.

## Test Steps
1. formatCents(4500) returns '$45.00'
2. formatCents(0) returns '$0.00'
3. parseDollars('$45.50') returns 4550
4. parseDollars('45') returns 4500
5. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'customer') = { customerTotal: 10500, vendorReceives: 10000, platformFee: 500 }
6. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'vendor') = { customerTotal: 10000, vendorReceives: 9500, platformFee: 500 }
7. calculatePlatformFee(10000, 500, 'split') = { customerTotal: 10250, vendorReceives: 9750, platformFee: 500 }

## Automated Checks

1. **Run type checking** - Check CLAUDE.md or package.json for commands (e.g., `bun typecheck`)

## If Verification FAILS

If the issue is trivial (typo, missing import, small syntax error):
1. Fix it directly

### Significant Issues (architectural, doesn't meet spec)

Do NOT reopen the implement step yourself - that's the orchestrator's job.

## Checklist

title: Commit Admin Catch Recording UI
description:
# COMMIT PHASE

## Goal
Create a clean git commit with the changes.

## Task Context
## Overview
Build the admin catch recording page (/admin/catch) with a massive dock-friendly mic button, integrate with the audio processing API (built in a prior task), add the dashboard nav card, and register the route. This is the full admin-side UI for voice-to-catch.

## Part 1: Admin Catch Page (Server Component)

### CatchPage.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchPage.tsx)
- Server component following existing pattern (see MarketConfigPage.tsx as reference)
- Auth check: hasAdminAccess(ctx), return appropriate error components if not
- Fetch latest live CatchUpdate for org: `db.catchUpdate.findFirst({ where: { organizationId: ctx.currentOrganization.id, status: "live" }, orderBy: { createdAt: "desc" } })`
- Pass currentCatch (or null) + ctx to CatchUI client component

## Part 2: Admin Catch UI (Client Component)

### CatchUI.tsx (src/app/pages/admin/catch/CatchUI.tsx)
- "use client" component
- State machine with states: idle | recording | processing | done | error | permission-denied

### Mic Button (centerpiece)
- 120px+ diameter circle, `border-radius: var(--radius-full)`
- Idle: ocean blue (`var(--color-action-primary)`), mic icon (SVG or emoji)
- Text below: "Tap to record what's fresh"
- Must be easy to tap with a big thumb on a dock

### Recording State
- Pulsing red ring animation (CSS keyframes)
- 2-minute countdown timer display (mm:ss format)
- Audio level indicator: use AudioContext + AnalyserNode
  - Connect MediaStream source to AnalyserNode
  - Read frequency data in requestAnimationFrame loop
  - Map amplitude to visual feedback (ring glow intensity or thickness pulsing with voice)
  - Evan needs to SEE the mic is picking up sound over dock wind/noise
- Auto-stop AND auto-submit at 0:00 (same behavior as manual stop)
- Text: "Recording... Tap to stop"

### MediaRecorder Integration
- MIME type detection: `MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported()`
  - Prefer `audio/webm;codecs=opus` (Chrome, Android)
  - Fallback: `audio/mp4` (Safari, iOS)
  - Fallback: `audio/webm`
- getUserMedia: `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: { echoCancellation: true, noiseSuppression: true } })`
- On stop: convert blob to ArrayBuffer, POST to /api/catch/record
- Release mic tracks on stop: `stream.getTracks().forEach(t => t.stop())`

### Error State (CRITICAL for dock use)
- RETAIN audio blob in memory on upload failure
- "Retry" button: re-uploads the SAME recording (no re-recording needed)
- "Re-record" button: separate, discards blob, returns to idle
- Error message displayed clearly
- Critical for spotty dock signal — don't lose a 2-minute recording on failure

### Permission Denied State
- Handle navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia rejection
- Show explanation: "Microphone access is needed to record your catch update"
- Instructions to re-enable in browser settings
- Retry button to request permission again

### Processing State
- Spinner animation
- Text: "Transcribing & formatting..."
- Non-interactive (no cancel, let it complete)

### Done State
- Green checkmark or success indicator
- Show formatted catch result (headline, item list, summary)
- "Re-record" button to start over
- "Clear catch" button to archive current live catch without replacing

### Current Live Catch Display
- Below the mic button area
- "Currently showing to customers:" header
- Render the current live catch data if exists
- Show "No catch currently displayed" if none

### Clear Catch
- Button that archives current live catch without recording new one
- Calls clearCatch() server function
- Use case: off-season, or Evan wants to stop showing catch

## Part 3: Server Functions (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch-functions.ts)
- "use server" module
- `clearCatch()`: archives current live catch for org, calls revalidatePath("/")
- Follow existing server function patterns: requestInfo, hasAdminAccess check, return { success, error? }

## Part 4: Styles (src/app/pages/admin/catch/catch.css)
- BEM-like naming: `.catch-mic`, `.catch-mic__ring`, `.catch-mic__ring--recording`, `.catch-mic__timer`, `.catch-result`, etc.
- Use semantic design tokens ONLY (var(--color-*), var(--space-*), var(--radius-*))
- @keyframes for pulse animation (recording state)
- @keyframes for amplitude visualization
- Mobile-first: big touch targets, readable on small screens
- @media (max-width: 640px) adjustments if needed

## Part 5: Dashboard Nav Card + Route Registration

### AdminDashboardUI.tsx
- Add 4th nav card to the existing grid:
  - Icon: 🎙️ (microphone emoji)
  - Title: "Fresh Catch"
  - Description: "Record what's available this week"
  - Link: /admin/catch

### routes.ts (src/app/pages/admin/routes.ts)
- Import CatchPage from ./catch/CatchPage
- Add: route("/catch", CatchPage)

2. See big mic button in idle state (120px+, ocean blue)
3. Tap mic — browser requests microphone permission
4. Grant permission — see recording state: red pulsing ring, countdown from 2:00
5. See audio level indicator responding to voice input (visual amplitude)
6. Tap again to stop — see processing spinner ("Transcribing & formatting...")
7. After processing: see formatted catch result with headline, items, summary

11. Tap "Retry" — re-uploads same audio without re-recording
12. Test "Clear catch" — archives current catch
13. Deny mic permission — see helpful permission-denied message with instructions
14. Navigate to /admin — see 4th "Fresh Catch" card with mic icon
15. Click "Fresh Catch" card — navigates to /admin/catch
16. Test on mobile viewport: mic button easy to tap with thumb
17. Verify all styles use semantic design tokens (no hardcoded hex)

If approaching 30-40% context usage with remaining work, create continuation beads for remaining items and exit cleanly.

title: Verify CommandBar FAB component + half-sheet
description:
# VERIFICATION PHASE

## Goal
Validate that the implementation works correctly through automated AND manual verification.

## Task Context
Create the floating action button (mic) and half-sheet recording UI that mounts on all admin pages.

Create src/components/CommandBar.tsx ("use client"):
- FAB: fixed position bottom-right (16px from edges on mobile), 56px circle, mic icon, z-index above content, shadow + hover lift
- Tap FAB → half-sheet slides up from bottom (CSS transition, max-height 70vh)
- Half-sheet contains:
  - VoiceMicButton (shared component from useVoiceRecorder)
  - Hint chips: contextual based on optional hintContext prop (catch page → "what's fresh today", markets page → "add market, create popup, update details")
  - Input mode toggle: Voice | Type (same pattern as CatchUI)
  - Text textarea fallback with hint text as placeholder
- Close triggers: Escape key, tap outside half-sheet, swipe down (optional)
- Props: onResult(result: VoiceCommandResult), hintContext?: string

Create src/components/command-bar.css:
- FAB: circular, var(--color-action-primary), var(--shadow-lg)
- Half-sheet: slides up, border-radius top corners (var(--radius-xl)), backdrop blur, var(--color-surface-primary) background
- Slide-up animation (transform translateY)
- Mobile: full-width half-sheet

Mount in AdminLayout (or admin route wrapper):
- Import and render CommandBar
- Wire onResult to state that will be consumed by CommandReview (next task)

## Test Steps
1. FAB visible on /admin/dashboard, /admin/config, /admin/catch, /admin/orders
2. Tap FAB → half-sheet slides up smoothly
3. VoiceMicButton renders inside half-sheet
4. Record audio → processing state shown
5. Text mode toggle works
6. Escape closes half-sheet
7. Tap outside closes half-sheet
8. FAB doesn't obscure page content or bottom nav
9. Mobile: full-width, thumb-reachable FAB position

Ensure all views handle price migration edge cases: null price shows 'Price pending' or dash. Sorting: null prices sort last. Filtering: null included in all, excluded from price filters. Print view: null shows TBD. No crashes on null/undefined. Test with mix of old and new orders.

2. Priced orders display correctly
3. Mixed list renders without errors
4. Null values sort last
5. Print view handles null
